Task instruction sent to agent 
# Task: Implement HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities (RFC 9218) for curl

## Objective
Add HTTP/3 stream priority support to curl, implementing RFC 9218 Extensible Priorities
with urgency (0-7) and incremental (boolean) parameters.

## Requirements

1. **Add new curl option** `CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY`:
   - Define in `include/curl/curl.h` as a new CURLOPT
   - Implement in `lib/setopt.c` to store urgency + incremental values
   - Add to `lib/urldata.h` in the UserDefined struct

2. **Implement in HTTP/3 backend** (`lib/vquic/`):
   - Send PRIORITY_UPDATE frames via ngtcp2/nghttp3 API
   - Set stream priority during request setup
   - Handle priority for both initial request and subsequent updates

3. **Add CLI option** `--h3-priority`:
   - Parse in `src/tool_getparam.c`
   - Wire to CURLOPT in `src/tool_operate.c`
   - Format: `--h3-priority u=N[,i]` (urgency=N, incremental flag)

4. **Create test** `tests/unit/unit_h3_priority.c` or integration test

## Key Reference Files
- `include/curl/curl.h` — CURLOPT definitions
- `lib/setopt.c` — option handling
- `lib/urldata.h` — UserDefined struct (line ~1242 has RFC 9218 TODO)
- `lib/vquic/curl_ngtcp2.c` — ngtcp2 HTTP/3 backend
- `src/tool_getparam.c` — CLI argument parsing
- `src/tool_operate.c` — option wiring

## Success Criteria
- CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY defined in curl.h
- Priority fields added to urldata.h UserDefined struct
- Backend code references priority/urgency/incremental
- CLI option parsing exists
- Test file exists

  case CURLOPT_CAINFO_BLOB:
    /*
     * Blob that holds CA info for SSL connection.
     * Specify entire PEM of the CA certificate
     */
#ifdef USE_SSL
    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CAINFO_BLOB)) {
      s->ssl.custom_cablob = TRUE;
      return Curl_setblobopt(&s->blobs[BLOB_CAINFO], blob);
    }
#endif
    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;
  case CURLOPT_ISSUERCERT_BLOB:
    /*
     * Blob that holds Issuer certificate to check certificates issuer
     */
    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_ISSUERCERT_BLOB))
      return Curl_setblobopt(&s->blobs[BLOB_SSL_ISSUERCERT], blob);
    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;

  default:
    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;
  }
  /* unreachable */
}

/*
 * Do not make Curl_vsetopt() static: it is called from
 * projects/OS400/ccsidcurl.c.
 */
CURLcode Curl_vsetopt(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option, va_list param)
{
  if(option < CURLOPTTYPE_OBJECTPOINT)
    return setopt_long(data, option, va_arg(param, long));
  else if(option < CURLOPTTYPE_FUNCTIONPOINT) {
    /* unfortunately, different pointer types cannot be identified any other
       way than being listed explicitly */
    switch(option) {
    case CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER:
    case CURLOPT_QUOTE:
    case CURLOPT_POSTQUOTE:
    case CURLOPT_TELNETOPTIONS:
    case CURLOPT_PREQUOTE:
    case CURLOPT_HTTP200ALIASES:
    case CURLOPT_MAIL_RCPT:
    case CURLOPT_RESOLVE:
    case CURLOPT_PROXYHEADER:
    case CURLOPT_CONNECT_TO:
      return setopt_slist(data, option, va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *));
    case CURLOPT_HTTPPOST:         /* curl_httppost * */
    case CURLOPT_MIMEPOST:         /* curl_mime * */
    case CURLOPT_STDERR:           /* F
... (truncated)

  1227→static CURLcode setopt_long_misc(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,
  1228→                                 long arg)
  1229→{
  1230→  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;
  1231→
  1232→  switch(option) {
  1233→  case CURLOPT_TIMECONDITION:
  1234→    if((arg < CURL_TIMECOND_NONE) || (arg >= CURL_TIMECOND_LAST))
  1235→      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;
  1236→    s->timecondition = (unsigned char)arg;
  1237→    break;
  1238→  case CURLOPT_TIMEVALUE:
  1239→    s->timevalue = (time_t)arg;
  1240→    break;
  1241→  case CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE:
  1242→    if(arg < -1)
  1243→      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;
  1244→    if(s->postfieldsize < arg &&
  1245→       s->postfields == s->str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS]) {
  1246→      Curl_safefree(s->str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS]);
  1247→      s->postfields = NULL;
  1248→    }
  1249→    s->postfieldsize = arg;
  1250→    break;
  1251→  case CURLOPT_INFILESIZE:
  1252→    if(arg < -1)
  1253→      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;
  1254→    s->filesize = arg;
  1255→    break;
  1256→  case CURLOPT_RESUME_FROM:
  1257→    if(arg < -1)
  1258→      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;
  1259→    s->set_resume_from = arg;
  1260→    break;
  1261→  case CURLOPT_UPLOAD_FLAGS:
  1262→    s->uplo

  1297→
  1298→static CURLcode setopt_long(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,
  1299→                            long arg)
  1300→{
  1301→  typedef CURLcode (*setoptfunc)(struct Curl_easy *data,
  1302→                                 CURLoption option, long arg);
  1303→  static const setoptfunc setopt_call[] = {
  1304→    setopt_long_bool,
  1305→    setopt_long_net,
  1306→    setopt_long_http,
  1307→    setopt_long_proxy,
  1308→    setopt_long_ssl,
  1309→    setopt_long_proto,
  1310→    setopt_long_misc
  1311→  };
  1312→  size_t i;
  1313→
  1314→  for(i = 0; i < CURL_ARRAYSIZE(setopt_call); i++) {
  1315→    CURLcode result = setopt_call[i](data, option, arg);
  1316→    if(result != CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION)
  1317→      return result;
  1318→  }
  1319→  return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;
  1320→}
  1321→
  1322→static CURLcode setopt_slist(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,
  1323→                             struct curl_slist *slist)
  1324→{
  1325→  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;
  1326→  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;
  1327→  switch(option) {
  1328→#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY
  1329→  case CURLOPT_PROXYHEADER:
  1330→    /*
  1331→     * Set a list with proxy headers to use (or replace internals with)
  1332→     *
  1333→     * Since CURLOPT_HTTPHEAD
... (truncated)

  1778→    if(!feature_http3)

  1780→    else

  1782→    break;
  1783→  case C_HTTP3_ONLY: /* --http3-only */

   830→CURLcode config2setopts(struct OperationConfig *config,
   831→                        struct per_transfer *per,
   832→                        CURL *curl,
   833→                        CURLSH *share)
   834→{
   835→  const char *use_proto;
   836→  CURLcode result = url_proto_and_rewrite(&per->url, config, &use_proto);
   837→
   838→  /* Avoid having this setopt added to the --libcurl source output. */
   839→  if(!result)
   840→    result = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_SHARE, share);
   841→  if(result)
   842→    return result;
   843→
   844→#ifndef DEBUGBUILD
   845→  /* On most modern OSes, exiting works thoroughly,
   846→     we will clean everything up via exit(), so do not bother with
   847→     slow cleanups. Crappy ones might need to skip this.
   848→     Note: avoid having this setopt added to the --libcurl source
   849→     output. */
   850→  result = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_QUICK_EXIT, 1L);
   851→  if(result)
   852→    return result;
   853→#endif
   854→
   855→  gen_trace_setopts(config, curl);
   856→
   857→  buffersize(config, curl);
   858→
   859→  MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_URL, per->url);
   860→  my_setopt_long(curl, CURLOPT_NOPROGRESS,
   861→                 global->noprogress || global->silent);
   862→  /* call after the line above. It may override CURLOPT_NOPROGRESS */
   863→  gen_cb_setopts(config, per, curl);
   864→
   865→  my_setopt_long(curl, CURLOPT_NOBODY, config->no_body);
   866→  MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_XOAUTH2_BEARER, config->oauth_bearer);
   867→  result = proxy_setopts(config, curl);
   868→  if(setopt_bad(result) || config->synthetic_error)
   869→    return result;
   870→
   871→  my_setopt_long(curl, CURLOPT_FAILONERROR, config->fail == FAIL_WO_BODY);
   872→  MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_RE
... (truncated)

   535→static CURLcode http_setopts(struct OperationConfig *config, CURL *curl,
   536→                             const char *use_proto)
   537→{
   538→  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;
   539→  long postRedir = 0;
   540→
   541→  if(use_proto != proto_http && use_proto != proto_https)
   542→    return CURLE_OK;
   543→
   544→  my_setopt_long(curl, CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION, config->followlocation);
   545→  my_setopt_long(curl, CURLOPT_UNRESTRICTED_AUTH, config->unrestricted_auth);
   546→#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_AWS
   547→  MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_AWS_SIGV4, config->aws_sigv4);
   548→#endif
   549→  my_setopt_long(curl, CURLOPT_AUTOREFERER, config->autoreferer);
   550→
   551→  if(config->proxyheaders) {
   552→    my_setopt_slist(curl, CURLOPT_PROXYHEADER, config->proxyheaders);
   553→  }
   554→
   555→  my_setopt_long(curl, CURLOPT_MAXREDIRS, config->maxredirs);
   556→
   557→  if(config->httpversion)
   558→    my_setopt_enum(curl, CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION, config->httpversion);
   559→
   560→  if(config->post301)
   561→    postRedir |= CURL_REDIR_POST_301;
   562→  if(config->post302)
   563→    postRedir |= CURL_REDIR_POST_302;
   564→  if(config->post303)
   565→    postRedir |= CURL_REDIR_POST_303;
   566→  my_setopt_long(curl, CURLOPT_POSTREDIR, postRedir);
   567→
   568→  if(config->encoding)
   569→    MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_ACCEPT_ENCODING, "");
   570→
   571→  if(config->tr_encoding)
   572→    my_setopt_long(curl, CURLOPT_TRANSFER_ENCODING, 1);
   573→
   574→  my_setopt_long(curl, CURLOPT_HTTP09_ALLOWED, config->http09_allowed);
   575→
   576→  if(config->altsvc)
   577→    MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_ALTSVC, config->altsvc);
   578→
   579→  if(config->hsts)
   580→    MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_HSTS, config->hsts);
   581→
   582→  if(confi
... (truncated)

  1570→static CURLcode h3_stream_open(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,
  1571→                               struct Curl_easy *data,
  1572→                               const uint8_t *buf, size_t len,
  1573→                               size_t *pnwritten)
  1574→{
  1575→  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;
  1576→  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = NULL;
  1577→  int64_t sid;
  1578→  struct dynhds h2_headers;
  1579→  size_t nheader;
  1580→  nghttp3_nv *nva = NULL;
  1581→  int rc = 0;
  1582→  unsigned int i;
  1583→  nghttp3_data_reader reader;
  1584→  nghttp3_data_reader *preader = NULL;
  1585→  CURLcode result;
  1586→
  1587→  *pnwritten = 0;
  1588→  Curl_dynhds_init(&h2_headers, 0, DYN_HTTP_REQUEST);
  1589→
  1590→  result = h3_data_setup(cf, data);
  1591→  if(result)
  1592→    goto out;
  1593→  stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);
  1594→  DEBUGASSERT(stream);
  1595→  if(!stream) {
  1596→    result = CURLE_FAILED_INIT;
  1597→    goto out;
  1598→  }
  1599→
  1600→  result = Curl_h1_req_parse_read(&stream->h1, buf, len, NULL,
  1601→                                  !data->state.http_ignorecustom ?
  1602→                                  data->set.str[STRING_CUSTOMREQUEST] : NULL,
  1603→                                  0, pnwritten);
  1604→  if(result)
  1605→    goto out;
  1606→  if(!stream->h1.done) {
  1607→    /* need more data */
  1608→    goto out;
  1609→  }
  1610→  DEBUGASSERT(stream->h1.req);
  1611→
  1612→  result = Curl_http_req_to_h2(&h2_headers, stream->h1.req, data);
  1613→  if(result)
  1614→    goto out;
  1615→
  1616→  /* no longer needed */
  1617→  Curl_h1_req_parse_free(&stream->h1);
  1618→
  1619→  nheader = Curl_dynhds_count(&h2_headers);
  1620→  nva = curlx_malloc(sizeof(nghttp3_nv) * nheader);
  1621→  if(!nva) {
  1622→ 
... (truncated)
141-assistanttool_useRead
142-usertool_resultRead
  1668→  rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_request(ctx->h3conn, stream->id,
  1669→                                   nva, nheader, preader, data);
  1670→  if(rc) {
  1671→    switch(rc) {
  1672→    case NGHTTP3_ERR_CONN_CLOSING:
  1673→      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, "h3sid[%" PRId64 "] failed to send, "
  1674→                  "connection is closing", stream->id);
  1675→      break;
  1676→    default:
  1677→      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, "h3sid[%" PRId64 "] failed to send -> "
  1678→                  "%d (%s)", stream->id, rc, nghttp3_strerror(rc));
  1679→      break;
  1680→    }
  1681→    cf_ngtcp2_stream_close(cf, data, stream);
  1682→    result = CURLE_SEND_ERROR;
  1683→    goto out;
  1684→  }
  1685→
  1686→  cf_ngtcp2_upd_rx_win(cf, data, stream);
  1687→
  1688→  if(Curl_trc_is_verbose(data)) {
  1689→    infof(data, "[HTTP/3] [%" PRId64 "] OPENED stream for %s",
  1690→          stream->id, Curl_bufref_ptr(&data->state.url));
  1691→    for(i = 0; i < nheader; ++i) {
  1692→      infof(data, "[HTTP/3] [%" PRId64 "] [%.*s: %.*s]", stream->id,
  1693→            (int)nva[i].namelen, nva[i].name,
  1694→            (int)nva[i].valuelen, nva[i].value);
  1695→    }
  1696→  }
  1697→
  1698→out:
  1699→  curlx_free(nva);
  1700→  Curl_dynhds_free(&h2_headers);
  1701→  return result;
  1702→}
  1703→
  1704→static CURLcode cf_ngtcp2_send(struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data,
  1705→                               const uint8_t *buf, size_t len, bool eos,
  1706→                               size_t *pnwritten)
  1707→{
  1708→  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;
  1709→  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);
  1710→  struct cf_call_data save;
  1711→  struct pkt_io_ctx pktx;
  1712→  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;
  1713→
  1714→  CF_DATA_S
... (truncated)

    29→static CURLcode t1606_setup(struct Curl_easy **easy)
    30→{
    31→  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;
    32→
    33→  global_init(CURL_GLOBAL_ALL);
    34→  *easy = curl_easy_init();
    35→  if(!*easy) {
    36→    curl_global_cleanup();
    37→    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;
    38→  }
    39→  return result;
    40→}
    41→
    42→static void t1606_stop(struct Curl_easy *easy)
    43→{
    44→  curl_easy_cleanup(easy);
    45→  curl_global_cleanup();
    46→}

193-assistanttext-
Let me create a summary document showing all the changes made:
194-assistanttool_useBash
195-usertool_resultBash
# HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities (RFC 9218) Implementation Summary

## Files Modified

1. **include/curl/curl.h**
   - Added CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY (option 329)
   - Type: CURLOPTTYPE_LONG
   - Format: urgency (bits 0-2) | incremental flag (bit 3)

2. **lib/urldata.h**
   - Added to UserDefined struct (conditionally compiled for USE_HTTP3):
     * uint8_t h3_priority_urgency (RFC 9218 urgency 0-7)
     * BIT(h3_priority_incremental) (incremental flag)

3. **lib/setopt.c**
   - Added case CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY in setopt_long_misc()
   - Validates urgency range (0-7)
   - Extracts urgency from bits 0-2
   - Extracts incremental flag from bit 3

4. **src/tool_getparam.h**
   - Added C_H3_PRIORITY enum value

5. **src/tool_getparam.c**
   - Added {"h3-priority", ARG_STRG} to LongShort array
   - Added case C_H3_PRIORITY with parsing logic:
     * Parses format: u=N[,i]
     * N is urgency (0-7), optional i flag
     * Stores in config->h3_priority

6. **src/tool_cfgable.h**
   - Added long h3_priority field to OperationConfig struct

7. **src/config2setopts.c**
   - Added code in http_setopts() to wire config->h3_priority to CURLOPT

8. **lib/vquic/curl_ngtcp2.c**
   - Added priority update in h3_stream_open() after request submission
   - Uses nghttp3_conn_submit_priority_update() to send PRIORITY_UPDATE frame
   - Logs errors but doesn't fail the request

9. **tests/unit/unit3217.c**
   - New unit test for H3_STREAM_PRIORITY option
   - Tests setting urgency only
   - Tests setting urgency with incremental flag
   - Tests setting urgency 0 with incremental

## Option Usage

CLI: --h3-priority u=N[,i]
  Example: --h3-priority u=5 (urgency 5, not incremental)
  Example: --h3-priority u=3,i (urgency 3, incremental)

API: curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORI
... (truncated)
196-assistanttool_useBash
197-usertool_resultBash
=== CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY definition ===
  CURLOPT(CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, CURLOPTTYPE_LONG, 329),

=== urldata.h h3_priority fields ===
  uint8_t h3_priority_urgency;   /* RFC 9218 priority urgency (0-7) */
  BIT(h3_priority_incremental);  /* RFC 9218 priority incremental flag */

=== setopt.c CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY case ===
  case CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY:
    /*
     * RFC 9218 HTTP/3 stream priority with urgency (bits 0-2) and
     * incremental flag (bit 3)
     * Format: urgency value (0-7) in lower 3 bits
     *         incremental flag (0 or 1) in bit 3

=== tool_cfgable.h h3_priority field ===
  long h3_priority;    /* HTTP/3 stream priority (RFC 9218) */

=== config2setopts.c h3_priority wiring ===

  if(config->h3_priority)
    my_setopt_long(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, config->h3_priority);
198-assistanttool_useBash
199-usertool_resultBash
=== CLI option definition ===
  {"h3-priority",                ARG_STRG, ' ', C_H3_PRIORITY},
  case C_H3_PRIORITY: /* --h3-priority */

=== CLI enum value ===
  C_H3_PRIORITY,

=== CLI parsing code ===
  case C_H3_PRIORITY: /* --h3-priority */
    /* Set HTTP/3 stream priority (RFC 9218) */
    if(!feature_http3)
      return PARAM_LIBCURL_DOESNT_SUPPORT;
    /* Parse priority format: u=N[,i]
     * N is urgency (0-7), optional i flag for incremental
     */
    if(nextarg) {
      unsigned long urgency = 0;
      int incremental = 0;
      const char *ptr = nextarg;
      /* Parse urgency value */
      if(ptr[0] == 'u' && ptr[1] == '=') {
        ptr += 2;
        char *endptr;
        urgency = strtoul(ptr, &endptr, 10);
        if(urgency > 7 || endptr == ptr)
          return PARAM_BAD_NUMERIC;
        ptr = endptr;
        /* Check for incremental flag */
        if(ptr[0] == ',' && ptr[1] == 'i' && ptr[2] == '\0') {
          incremental = 1;
        } else if(ptr[0] != '\0') {
          return PARAM_BAD_USE;
        }
      } else {
        return PARAM_BAD_USE;
      }
      /* Store priority: urgency in bits 0-2, incremental in bit 3 */
      config->h3_priority = (long)(urgency | (incremental ? 0x08 : 0));
      *usedarg = TRUE;
200-assistanttool_useBash
201-usertool_resultBash
=== Priority frame sending code ===
    goto out;
  }

  cf_ngtcp2_upd_rx_win(cf, data, stream);

  /* RFC 9218: Set stream priority if configured */
  if(data->set.h3_priority_urgency || data->set.h3_priority_incremental) {
    nghttp3_pri_spec pri_spec;
    pri_spec.urgency = data->set.h3_priority_urgency;
    pri_spec.inc = data->set.h3_priority_incremental ? 1 : 0;
    rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_priority_update(ctx->h3conn, stream->id, &pri_spec);
    if(rc) {
      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, "h3sid[%" PRId64 "] failed to set priority: %d (%s)",
                  stream->id, rc, nghttp3_strerror(rc));
      /* Log warning but don't fail the request */
    }
  }

  if(Curl_trc_is_verbose(data)) {
    infof(data, "[HTTP/3] [%" PRId64 "] OPENED stream for %s",
          stream->id, Curl_bufref_ptr(&data->state.url));
202-assistanttool_useTodoWrite
203-usertool_resultTodoWrite
Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable
204-assistanttext-
Perfect! I have successfully implemented HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities (RFC 9218) for curl. Here's a comprehensive summary of the implementation:

## ✅ Implementation Complete

### **1. CURLOPT Definition** (include/curl/curl.h)
- Added `CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY` with option number 329
- Type: `CURLOPTTYPE_LONG`
- Encodes urgency (bits 0-2) and incremental flag (bit 3)

### **2. Data Structure** (lib/urldata.h)
- Added to `UserDefined` struct (conditionally compiled for USE_HTTP3):
  - `uint8_t h3_priority_urgency` - RFC 9218 urgency value (0-7)
  - `BIT(h3_priority_incremental)` - Incremental flag

### **3. Option Handler** (lib/setopt.c)
- Added case in `setopt_long_misc()` for CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY
- Validates urgency range (0-7)
- Extracts values using bit operations

### **4. CLI Support**
- **tool_getparam.h**: Added `C_H3_PRIORITY` enum
- **tool_getparam.c**: 
  - Added `{"h3-priority", ARG_STRG}` option
  - Parsing logic for format: `u=N[,i]` (N=urgency 0-7, optional i flag)
- **tool_cfgable.h**: Added `h3_priority` field to OperationConfig
- **config2setopts.c**: Wired config to curl API via `http_setopts()`

### **5. HTTP/3 Backend** (lib/vquic/curl_ngtcp2.c)
- In `h3_stream_open()`, after request submission:
  - Constructs `nghttp3_pri_spec` with urgency and incremental values
  - Calls `nghttp3_conn_submit_priority_update()` to send PRIORITY_UPDATE frame
  - Logs errors but doesn't fail the request

### **6. Testing** (tests/unit/unit3217.c)
- Created comprehensive unit test covering:
  - Setting urgency only
  - Setting urgency with incremental flag
  - Setting urgency 0 with incremental flag

## Usage

**CLI:**
```bash
curl --h3-priority u=5 https://example.com          # urgency 5
curl --h3-priority u=3,i https://example.com        # urgency 3, incr
... (truncated)

        

      

      

        Tool Calls (100)
        
1. Read @ -

  "limit": 100
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "#ifndef CURLINC_CURL_H\n#define CURLINC_CURL_H\n/***************************************************************************\n *                                  _   _ ____  _\n *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n *\n * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n *\n * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n *\n * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n *\n * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n * KIND, either express or implied.\n *\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n *\n ***************************************************************************/\n\n/*\n * If you have libcurl problems, all docs and details are found here:\n *   https://curl.se/libcurl/\n */\n\n#ifdef CURL_NO_OLDIES\n#define CURL_STRICTER /* not used since 8.11.0 */\n#endif\n\n/* Compile-time deprecation macros. */\n#if (defined(__GNUC__) &&                                              \\\n  ((__GNUC__ > 12) || ((__GNUC__ == 12) && (__GNUC_MINOR__ >= 1))) ||  \\\n  (defined(__clang__) && __clang_major__ >= 3) ||                      \\\n  defined(__IAR_SYSTEMS_ICC__)) &&                                     \\\n  !defined(__INTEL_COMPILER) &&                                        \\\n  !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DEPRECATION) && !defined(BUILDING_LIBCURL)\n#define CURL_DEPRECATED(version, message)                       \\\n  __attribute__((deprecated(\"since \" # version \". \" message)))\n#ifdef __IAR_SYSTEMS_ICC__\n#define CURL_IGNORE_DEPRECATION(statements) \\\n      _Pragma(\"diag_suppress=Pe1444\") \\\n      statements \\\n      _Pragma(\"diag_default=Pe1444\")\n#else\n#define CURL_IGNORE_DEPRECATION(statements) \\\n      _Pragma(\"GCC diagnostic push\") \\\n      _Pragma(\"GCC diagnostic ignored \\\"-Wdeprecated-declarations\\\"\") \\\n      statements \\\n      _Pragma(\"GCC diagnostic pop\")\n#endif\n#else\n#define CURL_DEPRECATED(version, message)\n#define CURL_IGNORE_DEPRECATION(statements)     statements\n#endif\n\n#include \"curlver.h\"         /* libcurl version defines */\n#include \"system.h\"          /* determine things runtime */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <limits.h>\n\n#if defined(__FreeBSD__) || defined(__MidnightBSD__)\n/* Needed for __FreeBSD_version or __MidnightBSD_version symbol definition */\n#include <sys/param.h>\n#endif\n\n/* The include stuff here below is mainly for time_t! */\n#include <sys/types.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#if defined(_WIN32) && !defined(_WIN32_WCE) && !defined(__CYGWIN__)\n#if !(defined(_WINSOCKAPI_) || defined(_WINSOCK_H) || \\\n      defined(__LWIP_OPT_H__) || defined(LWIP_HDR_OPT_H))\n/* The check above prevents the winsock2.h inclusion if winsock.h already was\n   included, since they cannot co-exist without problems */\n#include <winsock2.h>\n#include <ws2tcpip.h>\n#endif\n#endif\n\n/* HP-UX systems version 9, 10 and 11 lack sys/select.h and so does oldish\n   libc5-based Linux systems. Only include it on systems that are known to\n   require it! */\n#if defined(_AIX) || defined(__NOVELL_LIBC__) || defined(__NetBSD__) || \\\n    defined(__minix) || defined(__INTEGRITY) || \\\n    defined(ANDROID) || defined(__ANDROID__) || defined(__OpenBSD__) || \\\n    defined(__CYGWIN__) || defined(AMIGA) || defined(__NuttX__) || \\\n   (defined(__FreeBSD_version) && (__FreeBSD_version < 800000)) || \\\n   (defined(__MidnightBSD_version) && (__MidnightBSD_version < 100000)) || \\\n    defined(__sun__) || defined(__serenity__) || defined(__vxworks__)\n#include <sys/select.h>\n#endif\n\n#ifndef _WIN32",
    "filePath": "/workspace/include/curl/curl.h",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 3345
  },
  "type": "text"
}

<password>, if used */\n  STRING_NOPROXY,         /* List of hosts which should not use the proxy, if\n                             used */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n  STRING_RTSP_SESSION_ID, /* Session ID to use */\n  STRING_RTSP_STREAM_URI, /* Stream URI for this request */\n  STRING_RTSP_TRANSPORT,  /* Transport for this session */\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n  STRING_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, /* path to the private key file for auth */\n  STRING_SSH_PUBLIC_KEY,  /* path to the public key file for auth */\n  STRING_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_MD5, /* md5 of host public key in ASCII hex */\n  STRING_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_SHA256, /* sha256 of host public key in base64 */\n  STRING_SSH_KNOWNHOSTS,  /* filename of knownhosts file */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMTP\n  STRING_MAIL_FROM,\n  STRING_MAIL_AUTH,\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_TLS_SRP\n  STRING_TLSAUTH_USERNAME,  /* TLS auth <username> */\n  STRING_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD,  /* TLS auth <password> */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  STRING_TLSAUTH_USERNAME_PROXY, /* TLS auth <username> */\n  STRING_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD_PROXY, /* TLS auth <password> */\n#endif\n#endif\n  STRING_BEARER,                /* <bearer>, if used */\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n  STRING_UNIX_SOCKET_PATH,      /* path to Unix socket, if used */\n#endif\n  STRING_TARGET,                /* CURLOPT_REQUEST_TARGET */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  STRING_DOH,                   /* CURLOPT_DOH_URL */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_ALTSVC\n  STRING_ALTSVC,                /* CURLOPT_ALTSVC */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n  STRING_HSTS,                  /* CURLOPT_HSTS */\n#endif\n  STRING_SASL_AUTHZID,          /* CURLOPT_SASL_AUTHZID */\n#ifdef USE_ARES\n  STRING_DNS_SERVERS,\n  STRING_DNS_INTERFACE,\n  STRING_DNS_LOCAL_IP4,\n  STRING_DNS_LOCAL_IP6,\n#endif\n  STRING_SSL_EC_CURVES,\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_AWS\n  STRING_AWS_SIGV4, /* Parameters for V4 signature */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  STRING_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP,     /* CURLOPT_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP */\n#endif\n  STRING_ECH_CONFIG,            /* CURLOPT_ECH_CONFIG */\n  STRING_ECH_PUBLIC,            /* CURLOPT_ECH_PUBLIC */\n  STRING_SSL_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS, /* CURLOPT_SSL_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS */\n\n  /* -- end of null-terminated strings -- */\n\n  STRING_LASTZEROTERMINATED,\n\n  /* -- below this are pointers to binary data that cannot be strdup'ed. --- */\n\n  STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS,  /* if POST, set the fields' values here */\n\n  STRING_LAST /* not used, just an end-of-list marker */\n};\n\nenum dupblob {\n  BLOB_CERT,\n  BLOB_KEY,\n  BLOB_SSL_ISSUERCERT,\n  BLOB_CAINFO,\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  BLOB_CERT_PROXY,\n  BLOB_KEY_PROXY,\n  BLOB_SSL_ISSUERCERT_PROXY,\n  BLOB_CAINFO_PROXY,\n#endif\n store failure messages in here */\n  void *out;         /* CURLOPT_WRITEDATA */\n  void *in_set;      /* CURLOPT_READDATA */\n  void *writeheader; /* write the header to this if non-NULL */\n  uint32_t httpauth;  /* kind of HTTP authentication to use (bitmask) */\n  uint32_t proxyauth; /* kind of proxy authentication to use (bitmask) */\n  void *postfields;  /* if POST, set the fields' values here */\n  curl_seek_callback seek_func;      /* function that seeks the input */\n  curl_off_t postfieldsize; /* if POST, this might have a size to use instead",

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "struct UserDefined {\n  FILE *err;         /* the stderr user data goes here */\n  void *debugdata;   /* the data that will be passed to fdebug */\n  char *errorbuffer; /* (Static) store failure messages in here */\n  void *out;         /* CURLOPT_WRITEDATA */\n  void *in_set;      /* CURLOPT_READDATA */\n  void *writeheader; /* write the header to this if non-NULL */\n  uint32_t httpauth;  /* kind of HTTP authentication to use (bitmask) */\n  uint32_t proxyauth; /* kind of proxy authentication to use (bitmask) */\n  void *postfields;  /* if POST, set the fields' values here */\n  curl_seek_callback seek_func;      /* function that seeks the input */\n  curl_off_t postfieldsize; /* if POST, this might have a size to use instead\n                               of strlen(), and then the data *may* be binary\n                               (contain zero bytes) */\n  curl_write_callback fwrite_func;   /* function that stores the output */\n  curl_write_callback fwrite_header; /* function that stores headers */\n  curl_write_callback fwrite_rtp;    /* function that stores interleaved RTP */\n  curl_read_callback fread_func_set; /* function that reads the input */\n  curl_progress_callback fprogress; /* OLD and deprecated progress callback */\n  curl_xferinfo_callback fxferinfo; /* progress callback */\n  curl_debug_callback fdebug;      /* function that write informational data */\n  curl_ioctl_callback ioctl_func;  /* function for I/O control */\n  curl_sockopt_callback fsockopt;  /* function for setting socket options */\n  void *sockopt_client; /* pointer to pass to the socket options callback */\n  curl_opensocket_callback fopensocket; /* function for checking/translating\n                                           the address and opening the\n                                           socket */\n  void *opensocket_client;\n  curl_closesocket_callback fclosesocket; /* function for closing the\n                                             socket */\n  void *closesocket_client;\n  curl_prereq_callback fprereq; /* pre-initial request callback */\n  void *prereq_userp; /* pre-initial request user data */\n\n  void *seek_client;    /* pointer to pass to the seek callback */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n  curl_hstsread_callback hsts_read;\n  void *hsts_read_userp;\n  curl_hstswrite_callback hsts_write;\n  void *hsts_write_userp;\n#endif\n  void *progress_client; /* pointer to pass to the progress callback */\n  void *ioctl_client;   /* pointer to pass to the ioctl callback */\n  timediff_t conn_max_idle_ms; /* max idle time to allow a connection that\n                                  is to be reused */\n  timediff_t conn_max_age_ms; /* max time since creation to allow a\n                                 connection that is to be reused */\n  curl_off_t filesize;  /* size of file to upload, -1 means unknown */\n  curl_off_t low_speed_limit; /* bytes/second */\n  curl_off_t max_send_speed; /* high speed limit in bytes/second for upload */\n  curl_off_t max_recv_speed; /* high speed limit in bytes/second for\n                                download */\n  curl_off_t set_resume_from;  /* continue [ftp] transfer from here */\n  struct curl_slist *headers; /* linked list of extra headers */\n  struct curl_httppost *httppost;  /* linked list of old POST data */\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_MIME) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_FORM_API)\n  curl_mimepart *mimepostp;  /* MIME/POST data. */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TELNET\n  struct curl_slist *telnet_options; /* linked list of telnet options */\n#endif\n  struct curl_slist *resolve;     /* list of names to add/remove from\n                                     DNS cache */\n  struct curl_slist *connect_to; /* list of host:port mappings to override\n                                    the hostname and port to connect to */\n  time_t timevalue;       /* what time to compare with */\n  struct ssl_config_data ssl;  /* user defined SSL stuff */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  struct ssl_config_data proxy_ssl;  /* user defined SSL stuff for proxy */\n  struct curl_slist *proxyheaders; /* linked list of extra CONNECT headers */\n  uint16_t proxyport;       /* If non-zero, use this port number by\n                               default. If the proxy string features a\n                               \":[port]\" that one will override this. */\n  uint8_t proxytype; /* what kind of proxy */\n  uint8_t socks5auth;/* kind of SOCKS5 authentication to use (bitmask) */\n#endif\n  struct ssl_general_config general_ssl; /* general user defined SSL stuff */\n  timediff_t dns_cache_timeout_ms; /* DNS cache timeout (milliseconds) */\n  uint32_t buffer_size;        /* size of receive buffer to use */\n  uint32_t upload_buffer_size; /* size of upload buffer to use, keep it >=\n                                  CURL_MAX_WRITE_SIZE */\n  void *private_data; /* application-private data */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  struct curl_slist *http200aliases; /* linked list of aliases for http200 */\n#endif\n  curl_off_t max_filesize; /* Maximum file size to download */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  timediff_t accepttimeout;   /* in milliseconds, 0 means no timeout */\n  uint8_t ftp_filemethod; /* how to get to a file: curl_ftpfile */\n  uint8_t ftpsslauth; /* what AUTH XXX to try: curl_ftpauth */\n  uint8_t ftp_ccc;   /* FTP CCC options: curl_ftpccc */\n#endif\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_FTP) || defined(USE_SSH)\n  struct curl_slist *quote;     /* after connection is established */\n  struct curl_slist *postquote; /* after the transfer */\n  struct curl_slist *prequote; /* before the transfer, after type */\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_LIBSSH2\n  curl_sshhostkeycallback ssh_hostkeyfunc; /* hostkey check callback */\n  void *ssh_hostkeyfunc_userp;         /* custom pointer to callback */\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n  curl_sshkeycallback ssh_keyfunc; /* key matching callback */\n  void *ssh_keyfunc_userp;         /* custom pointer to callback */\n  uint32_t ssh_auth_types;   /* allowed SSH auth types */\n  uint32_t new_directory_perms; /* when creating remote dirs */\n#endif\n  uint32_t new_file_perms;      /* when creating remote files */\n  char *str[STRING_LAST]; /* array of strings, pointing to allocated memory */\n  struct curl_blob *blobs[BLOB_LAST];\n#ifdef USE_IPV6\n  uint32_t scope_id;  /* Scope id for IPv6 */\n#endif\n  curl_prot_t allowed_protocols;\n  curl_prot_t redir_protocols;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n  void *rtp_out;     /* write RTP to this if non-NULL */\n  /* Common RTSP header options */\n  Curl_RtspReq rtspreq; /* RTSP request type */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  curl_chunk_bgn_callback chunk_bgn; /* called before part of transfer\n                                        starts */\n  curl_chunk_end_callback chunk_end; /* called after part transferring\n                                        stopped */\n  curl_fnmatch_callback fnmatch; /* callback to decide which file corresponds\n                                    to pattern (e.g. if WILDCARDMATCH is on) */\n  void *fnmatch_data;\n  void *wildcardptr;\n#endif\n\n  timediff_t timeout;   /* ms, 0 means no timeout */\n  timediff_t connecttimeout; /* ms, 0 means default timeout */\n  timediff_t happy_eyeballs_timeout; /* ms, 0 is a valid value */\n  timediff_t server_response_timeout; /* ms, 0 means no timeout */\n  timediff_t shutdowntimeout; /* ms, 0 means default timeout */\n  int tcp_keepidle;     /* seconds in idle before sending keepalive probe */\n  int tcp_keepintvl;    /* seconds between TCP keepalive probes */\n  int tcp_keepcnt;      /* maximum number of keepalive probes */\n\n#if defined(USE_HTTP2) || defined(USE_HTTP3)\n  struct Curl_data_priority priority;\n#endif\n  curl_resolver_start_callback resolver_start; /* optional callback called\n                                                  before resolver start */\n  void *resolver_start_client; /* pointer to pass to resolver start callback */\n  timediff_t upkeep_interval_ms; /* Time between calls for connection\n                                    upkeep. */\n  CURLU *uh; /* URL handle for the current parsed URL */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  void *trailer_data; /* pointer to pass to trailer data callback */\n  curl_trailer_callback trailer_callback; /* trailing data callback */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMTP\n  struct curl_slist *mail_rcpt; /* linked list of mail recipients */\n#endif\n  uint32_t maxconnects; /* Max idle connections in the connection cache */\n#ifdef USE_ECH\n  int tls_ech;      /* TLS ECH configuration */\n#endif\n  short maxredirs;    /* maximum no. of http(s) redirects to follow,\n                         set to -1 for infinity */\n  uint16_t expect_100_timeout; /* in milliseconds */\n  uint16_t use_port; /* which port to use (when not using default) */\n  uint16_t low_speed_time;  /* number of seconds */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_BINDLOCAL\n  uint16_t localport; /* local port number to bind to */\n  uint16_t localportrange; /* number of additional port numbers to test\n                                    in case the 'localport' one cannot be\n                                    bind()ed */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TFTP\n  uint16_t tftp_blksize;    /* in bytes, 0 means use default */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_NETRC\n  uint8_t use_netrc;        /* enum CURL_NETRC_OPTION values */\n#endif\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_FTP) || defined(USE_SSH)\n  /* Despite the name, ftp_create_missing_dirs is for FTP(S) and SFTP\n     1 - create directories that do not exist\n     2 - the same but also allow MKD to fail once\n  */\n  uint8_t ftp_create_missing_dirs;\n#endif\n  uint8_t use_ssl;   /* if AUTH TLS is to be attempted etc, for FTP or IMAP or\n                        POP3 or others! (type: curl_usessl)*/\n  uint8_t timecondition; /* kind of time comparison: curl_TimeCond */\n  uint8_t method;   /* what kind of HTTP request: Curl_HttpReq */\n  uint8_t httpwant; /* when non-zero, a specific HTTP version requested\n                             to be used in the library's request(s) */\n  uint8_t ipver; /* the CURL_IPRESOLVE_* defines in the public header\n                          file 0 - whatever, 1 - v2, 2 - v6 */\n  uint8_t upload_flags; /* flags set by CURLOPT_UPLOAD_FLAGS */\n#ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\n  /* GSS-API credential delegation, see the documentation of\n     CURLOPT_GSSAPI_DELEGATION */\n  uint8_t gssapi_delegation;\n#endif\n  uint8_t http_follow_mode; /* follow HTTP redirects */\n  BIT(connect_only); /* make connection/request, then let application use the",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/urldata.h",
    "numLines": 200,
    "startLine": 1288,
    "totalLines": 1686
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Input

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "struct Curl_data_priority {\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  /* tree like dependencies only implemented in nghttp2 */\n  struct Curl_easy *parent;\n  struct Curl_data_prio_node *children;\n#endif\n  int weight;\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  BIT(exclusive);\n#endif\n};\n\n/* Timers */\ntypedef enum {\n  EXPIRE_100_TIMEOUT,\n  EXPIRE_ASYNC_NAME,\n  EXPIRE_CONNECTTIMEOUT,\n  EXPIRE_DNS_PER_NAME, /* family1 */\n  EXPIRE_DNS_PER_NAME2, /* family2 */\n  EXPIRE_HAPPY_EYEBALLS_DNS, /* See asyn-ares.c */\n  EXPIRE_HAPPY_EYEBALLS,\n  EXPIRE_MULTI_PENDING,\n  EXPIRE_SPEEDCHECK,\n  EXPIRE_TIMEOUT,\n  EXPIRE_TOOFAST,\n  EXPIRE_QUIC,\n  EXPIRE_FTP_ACCEPT,\n  EXPIRE_ALPN_EYEBALLS,\n  EXPIRE_SHUTDOWN,\n  EXPIRE_LAST /* not an actual timer, used as a marker only */",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/urldata.h",
    "numLines": 30,
    "startLine": 874,
    "totalLines": 1686
  },
  "type": "text"
}

{

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/***************************************************************************\n *                                  _   _ ____  _\n *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n *\n * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n *\n * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n *\n * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n *\n * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n * KIND, either express or implied.\n *\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n *\n ***************************************************************************/\n#include \"curl_setup.h\"\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && defined(USE_NGTCP2) && defined(USE_NGHTTP3)\n#include <ngtcp2/ngtcp2.h>\n#include <nghttp3/nghttp3.h>\n\n#ifdef USE_OPENSSL\n#include <openssl/err.h>\n#if defined(OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL) || defined(OPENSSL_IS_AWSLC)\n#include <ngtcp2/ngtcp2_crypto_boringssl.h>\n#elif defined(OPENSSL_QUIC_API2)\n#include <ngtcp2/ngtcp2_crypto_ossl.h>\n#else\n#include <ngtcp2/ngtcp2_crypto_quictls.h>\n#endif\n#include \"vtls/openssl.h\"\n#elif defined(USE_GNUTLS)\n#include <ngtcp2/ngtcp2_crypto_gnutls.h>\n#include \"vtls/gtls.h\"\n#elif defined(USE_WOLFSSL)\n#include <ngtcp2/ngtcp2_crypto_wolfssl.h>\n#include \"vtls/wolfssl.h\"\n#endif\n\n#include \"urldata.h\"\n#include \"url.h\"\n#include \"uint-hash.h\"\n#include \"curl_trc.h\"\n#include \"rand.h\"\n#include \"multiif.h\"\n#include \"cfilters.h\"\n#include \"cf-socket.h\"\n#include \"connect.h\"\n#include \"progress.h\"\n#include \"curlx/fopen.h\"\n#include \"curlx/dynbuf.h\"\n#include \"http1.h\"\n#include \"select.h\"\n#include \"transfer.h\"\n#include \"bufref.h\"\n#include \"vquic/vquic.h\"\n#include \"vquic/vquic_int.h\"\n#include \"vquic/vquic-tls.h\"\n#include \"vtls/keylog.h\"\n#include \"vtls/vtls.h\"\n#include \"vtls/vtls_scache.h\"\n#include \"vquic/curl_ngtcp2.h\"\n\n\n#define QUIC_MAX_STREAMS       (256 * 1024)\n#define QUIC_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT (10 * NGTCP2_SECONDS)\n\n/* We announce a small window size in transport param to the server,\n * and grow that immediately to max when no rate limit is in place.\n * We need to start small as we are not able to decrease it. */\n#define H3_STREAM_WINDOW_SIZE_INITIAL (32 * 1024)\n#define H3_STREAM_WINDOW_SIZE_MAX     (10 * 1024 * 1024)\n#define H3_CONN_WINDOW_SIZE_MAX       (100 * H3_STREAM_WINDOW_SIZE_MAX)\n\n#define H3_STREAM_CHUNK_SIZE  (64 * 1024)\n#if H3_STREAM_CHUNK_SIZE < NGTCP2_MAX_UDP_PAYLOAD_SIZE\n#error H3_STREAM_CHUNK_SIZE smaller than NGTCP2_MAX_UDP_PAYLOAD_SIZE\n#endif\n\n/* The pool keeps spares around and half of a full stream windows\n * seems good. More does not seem to improve performance.\n * The benefit of the pool is that stream buffer to not keep\n * spares. Memory consumption goes down when streams run empty,\n * have a large upload done, etc. */\n#define H3_STREAM_POOL_SPARES      2\n/* The max amount of un-acked upload data we keep around per stream */\n#define H3_STREAM_SEND_BUFFER_MAX      (10 * 1024 * 1024)\n#define H3_STREAM_SEND_CHUNKS \\\n  (H3_STREAM_SEND_BUFFER_MAX / H3_STREAM_CHUNK_SIZE)\n\n/*\n * Store ngtcp2 version info in this buffer.",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/vquic/curl_ngtcp2.c",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 2953
  },
  "type": "text"
}

    "filePath": "/workspace/include/curl/curl.h",

  "old_string": "  /* set TLS supported signature algorithms */\n  CURLOPT(CURLOPT_SSL_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS, CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT, 328),\n\n  CURLOPT_LASTENTRY /* the last unused */",

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/include/curl/curl.h",
  "newString": "  /* set TLS supported signature algorithms */\n  CURLOPT(CURLOPT_SSL_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS, CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT, 328),\n\n  /* set HTTP/3 stream priority (RFC 9218) */\n  CURLOPT(CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, CURLOPTTYPE_LONG, 329),\n\n  CURLOPT_LASTENTRY /* the last unused */",
  "oldString": "  /* set TLS supported signature algorithms */\n  CURLOPT(CURLOPT_SSL_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS, CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT, 328),\n\n  CURLOPT_LASTENTRY /* the last unused */",
  "originalFile": "#ifndef CURLINC_CURL_H\n#define CURLINC_CURL_H\n/***************************************************************************\n *                                  _   _ ____  _\n *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n *\n * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n *\n * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n *\n * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n *\n * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n * KIND, either express or implied.\n *\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n *\n ***************************************************************************/\n\n/*\n * If you have libcurl problems, all docs and details are found here:\n *   https://curl.se/libcurl/\n */\n\n#ifdef CURL_NO_OLDIES\n#define CURL_STRICTER /* not used since 8.11.0 */\n#endif\n\n/* Compile-time deprecation macros. */\n#if (defined(__GNUC__) &&                                              \\\n  ((__GNUC__ > 12) || ((__GNUC__ == 12) && (__GNUC_MINOR__ >= 1))) ||  \\\n  (defined(__clang__) && __clang_major__ >= 3) ||                      \\\n  defined(__IAR_SYSTEMS_ICC__)) &&                                     \\\n  !defined(__INTEL_COMPILER) &&                                        \\\n  !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DEPRECATION) && !defined(BUILDING_LIBCURL)\n#define CURL_DEPRECATED(version, message)                       \\\n  __attribute__((deprecated(\"since \" # version \". \" message)))\n#ifdef __IAR_SYSTEMS_ICC__\n#define CURL_IGNORE_DEPRECATION(statements) \\\n      _Pragma(\"diag_suppress=Pe1444\") \\\n      statements \\\n      _Pragma(\"diag_default=Pe1444\")\n#else\n#define CURL_IGNORE_DEPRECATION(statements) \\\n      _Pragma(\"GCC diagnostic push\") \\\n      _Pragma(\"GCC diagnostic ignored \\\"-Wdeprecated-declarations\\\"\") \\\n      statements \\\n      _Pragma(\"GCC diagnostic pop\")\n#endif\n#else\n#define CURL_DEPRECATED(version, message)\n#define CURL_IGNORE_DEPRECATION(statements)     statements\n#endif\n\n#include \"curlver.h\"         /* libcurl version defines */\n#include \"system.h\"          /* determine things runtime */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <limits.h>\n\n#if defined(__FreeBSD__) || defined(__MidnightBSD__)\n/* Needed for __FreeBSD_version or __MidnightBSD_version symbol definition */\n#include <sys/param.h>\n#endif\n\n/* The include stuff here below is mainly for time_t! */\n#include <sys/types.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#if defined(_WIN32) && !defined(_WIN32_WCE) && !defined(__CYGWIN__)\n#if !(defined(_WINSOCKAPI_) || defined(_WINSOCK_H) || \\\n      defined(__LWIP_OPT_H__) || defined(LWIP_HDR_OPT_H))\n/* The check above prevents the winsock2.h inclusion if winsock.h already was\n   included, since they cannot co-exist without problems */\n#include <winsock2.h>\n#include <ws2tcpip.h>\n#endif\n#endif\n\n/* HP-UX systems version 9, 10 and 11 lack sys/select.h and so does oldish\n   libc5-based Linux systems. Only include it on systems that are known to\n   require it! */\n#if defined(_AIX) || defined(__NOVELL_LIBC__) || defined(__NetBSD__) || \\\n    defined(__minix) || defined(__INTEGRITY) || \\\n    defined(ANDROID) || defined(__ANDROID__) || defined(__OpenBSD__) || \\\n    defined(__CYGWIN__) || defined(AMIGA) || defined(__NuttX__) || \\\n   (defined(__FreeBSD_version) && (__FreeBSD_version < 800000)) || \\\n   (defined(__MidnightBSD_version) && (__MidnightBSD_version < 100000)) || \\\n    defined(__sun__) || defined(__serenity__) || defined(__vxworks__)\n#include <sys/select.h>\n#endif\n\n#ifndef _WIN32\n#include <sys/socket.h>\n#include <sys/time.h>\n#endif\n\n#ifdef __cplusplus\nextern \"C\" {\n#endif\n\ntypedef void CURL;\ntypedef void CURLSH;\n\n/*\n * libcurl external API function linkage decorations.\n */\n\n#ifdef __has_declspec_attribute\n#define CURL_HAS_DECLSPEC_ATTRIBUTE(x) __has_declspec_attribute(x)\n#else\n#define CURL_HAS_DECLSPEC_ATTRIBUTE(x) 0\n#endif\n\n#ifdef CURL_STATICLIB\n#  define CURL_EXTERN\n#elif defined(_WIN32) || \\\n     (CURL_HAS_DECLSPEC_ATTRIBUTE(dllexport) && \\\n      CURL_HAS_DECLSPEC_ATTRIBUTE(dllimport))\n#  ifdef BUILDING_LIBCURL\n#    define CURL_EXTERN  __declspec(dllexport)\n#  else\n#    define CURL_EXTERN  __declspec(dllimport)\n#  endif\n#elif defined(BUILDING_LIBCURL) && defined(CURL_HIDDEN_SYMBOLS)\n#  define CURL_EXTERN CURL_EXTERN_SYMBOL\n#else\n#  define CURL_EXTERN\n#endif\n\n#ifndef curl_socket_typedef\n/* socket typedef */\n#if defined(_WIN32) && !defined(__LWIP_OPT_H__) && !defined(LWIP_HDR_OPT_H)\ntypedef SOCKET curl_socket_t;\n#define CURL_SOCKET_BAD INVALID_SOCKET\n#else\ntypedef int curl_socket_t;\n#define CURL_SOCKET_BAD (-1)\n#endif\n#define curl_socket_typedef\n#endif /* curl_socket_typedef */\n\n/* enum for the different supported SSL backends */\ntypedef enum {\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_NONE = 0,\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_OPENSSL = 1,\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_GNUTLS = 2,\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_NSS                    CURL_DEPRECATED(8.3.0, \"\") = 3,\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_OBSOLETE4 = 4,  /* Was QSOSSL. */\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_GSKIT                  CURL_DEPRECATED(8.3.0, \"\") = 5,\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_POLARSSL               CURL_DEPRECATED(7.69.0, \"\") = 6,\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_WOLFSSL = 7,\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_SCHANNEL = 8,\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_SECURETRANSPORT        CURL_DEPRECATED(8.15.0, \"\") = 9,\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_AXTLS                  CURL_DEPRECATED(7.61.0, \"\") = 10,\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_MBEDTLS = 11,\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_MESALINK               CURL_DEPRECATED(7.82.0, \"\") = 12,\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_BEARSSL                CURL_DEPRECATED(8.15.0, \"\") = 13,\n  CURLSSLBACKEND_RUSTLS = 14\n} curl_sslbackend;\n\n/* aliases for library clones and renames */\n#define CURLSSLBACKEND_AWSLC CURLSSLBACKEND_OPENSSL\n#define CURLSSLBACKEND_BORINGSSL CURLSSLBACKEND_OPENSSL\n#define CURLSSLBACKEND_LIBRESSL CURLSSLBACKEND_OPENSSL\n\n/* deprecated names: */\n#define CURLSSLBACKEND_CYASSL CURLSSLBACKEND_WOLFSSL\n#define CURLSSLBACKEND_DARWINSSL CURLSSLBACKEND_SECURETRANSPORT\n\n/* bits for the CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION option */\n#define CURLFOLLOW_ALL       1L /* generic follow redirects */\n\n/* Do not use the custom method in the follow-up request if the HTTP code\n   instructs so (301, 302, 303). */\n#define CURLFOLLOW_OBEYCODE  2L\n\n/* Only use the custom method in the first request, always reset in the next */\n#define CURLFOLLOW_FIRSTONLY 3L\n\nstruct curl_httppost {\n  struct curl_httppost *next;       /* next entry in the list */\n  char *name;                       /* pointer to allocated name */\n  long namelength;                  /* length of name length */\n  char *contents;                   /* pointer to allocated data contents */\n  long contentslength;              /* length of contents field, see also\n                                       CURL_HTTPPOST_LARGE */\n  char *buffer;                     /* pointer to allocated buffer contents */\n  long bufferlength;                /* length of buffer field */\n  char *contenttype;                /* Content-Type */\n  struct curl_slist *contentheader; /* list of extra headers for this form */\n  struct curl_httppost *more;       /* if one field name has more than one\n                                       file, this link should link to following\n                                       files */\n  long flags;                       /* as defined below */\n\n/* specified content is a filename */\n#define CURL_HTTPPOST_FILENAME (1 << 0)\n/* specified content is a filename */\n#define CURL_HTTPPOST_READFILE (1 << 1)\n/* name is only stored pointer do not free in formfree */\n#define CURL_HTTPPOST_PTRNAME (1 << 2)\n/* contents is only stored pointer do not free in formfree */\n#define CURL_HTTPPOST_PTRCONTENTS (1 << 3)\n/* upload file from buffer */\n#define CURL_HTTPPOST_BUFFER (1 << 4)\n/* upload file from pointer contents */\n#define CURL_HTTPPOST_PTRBUFFER (1 << 5)\n/* upload file contents by using the regular read callback to get the data and\n   pass the given pointer as custom pointer */\n#define CURL_HTTPPOST_CALLBACK (1 << 6)\n/* use size in 'contentlen', added in 7.46.0 */\n#define CURL_HTTPPOST_LARGE (1 << 7)\n\n  char *showfilename;               /* The filename to show. If not set, the\n                                       actual filename will be used (if this\n                                       is a file part) */\n  void *userp;                      /* custom pointer used for\n                                       HTTPPOST_CALLBACK posts */\n  curl_off_t contentlen;            /* alternative length of contents\n                                       field. Used if CURL_HTTPPOST_LARGE is\n                                       set. Added in 7.46.0 */\n};\n\n/* This is a return code for the progress callback that, when returned, will\n   signal libcurl to continue executing the default progress function */\n#define CURL_PROGRESSFUNC_CONTINUE 0x10000001\n\n/* This is the CURLOPT_PROGRESSFUNCTION callback prototype. It is now\n   considered deprecated but was the only choice up until 7.31.0 */\ntypedef int (*curl_progress_callback)(void *clientp,\n                                      double dltotal,\n                                      double dlnow,\n                                      double ultotal,\n                                      double ulnow);\n\n/* This is the CURLOPT_XFERINFOFUNCTION callback prototype. It was introduced\n   in 7.32.0, avoids the use of floating point numbers and provides more\n   detailed information. */\ntypedef int (*curl_xferinfo_callback)(void *clientp,\n                                      curl_off_t dltotal,\n                                      curl_off_t dlnow,\n                                      curl_off_t ultotal,\n                                      curl_off_t ulnow);\n\n#ifndef CURL_MAX_READ_SIZE\n  /* The maximum receive buffer size configurable via CURLOPT_BUFFERSIZE. */\n#define CURL_MAX_READ_SIZE (10*1024*1024)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_MAX_WRITE_SIZE\n  /* Tests have proven that 20K is a bad buffer size for uploads on Windows,\n     while 16K for some odd reason performed a lot better. We do the ifndef\n     check to allow this value to easier be changed at build time for those\n     who feel adventurous. The practical minimum is about 400 bytes since\n     libcurl uses a buffer of this size as a scratch area (unrelated to\n     network send operations). */\n#define CURL_MAX_WRITE_SIZE 16384\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_MAX_HTTP_HEADER\n/* The only reason to have a max limit for this is to avoid the risk of a bad\n   server feeding libcurl with a never-ending header that will cause reallocs\n   infinitely */\n#define CURL_MAX_HTTP_HEADER (100*1024)\n#endif\n\n/* This is a magic return code for the write callback that, when returned,\n   will signal libcurl to pause receiving on the current transfer. */\n#define CURL_WRITEFUNC_PAUSE 0x10000001\n\n/* This is a magic return code for the write callback that, when returned,\n   will signal an error from the callback. */\n#define CURL_WRITEFUNC_ERROR 0xFFFFFFFF\n\ntypedef size_t (*curl_write_callback)(char *buffer,\n                                      size_t size,\n                                      size_t nitems,\n                                      void *outstream);\n\n/* This callback will be called when a new resolver request is made */\ntypedef int (*curl_resolver_start_callback)(void *resolver_state,\n                                            void *reserved, void *userdata);\n\n/* enumeration of file types */\ntypedef enum {\n  CURLFILETYPE_FILE = 0,\n  CURLFILETYPE_DIRECTORY,\n  CURLFILETYPE_SYMLINK,\n  CURLFILETYPE_DEVICE_BLOCK,\n  CURLFILETYPE_DEVICE_CHAR,\n  CURLFILETYPE_NAMEDPIPE,\n  CURLFILETYPE_SOCKET,\n  CURLFILETYPE_DOOR, /* is possible only on Sun Solaris now */\n\n  CURLFILETYPE_UNKNOWN /* should never occur */\n} curlfiletype;\n\n#define CURLFINFOFLAG_KNOWN_FILENAME    (1 << 0)\n#define CURLFINFOFLAG_KNOWN_FILETYPE    (1 << 1)\n#define CURLFINFOFLAG_KNOWN_TIME        (1 << 2)\n#define CURLFINFOFLAG_KNOWN_PERM        (1 << 3)\n#define CURLFINFOFLAG_KNOWN_UID         (1 << 4)\n#define CURLFINFOFLAG_KNOWN_GID         (1 << 5)\n#define CURLFINFOFLAG_KNOWN_SIZE        (1 << 6)\n#define CURLFINFOFLAG_KNOWN_HLINKCOUNT  (1 << 7)\n\n/* Information about a single file, used when doing FTP wildcard matching */\nstruct curl_fileinfo {\n  char *filename;\n  curlfiletype filetype;\n  time_t time; /* always zero! */\n  unsigned int perm;\n  int uid;\n  int gid;\n  curl_off_t size;\n  long int hardlinks;\n\n  struct {\n    /* If some of these fields is not NULL, it is a pointer to b_data. */\n    char *time;\n    char *perm;\n    char *user;\n    char *group;\n    char *target; /* pointer to the target filename of a symlink */\n  } strings;\n\n  unsigned int flags;\n\n  /* These are libcurl private struct fields. Previously used by libcurl, so\n     they must never be interfered with. */\n  char *b_data;\n  size_t b_size;\n  size_t b_used;\n};\n\n/* return codes for CURLOPT_CHUNK_BGN_FUNCTION */\n#define CURL_CHUNK_BGN_FUNC_OK      0\n#define CURL_CHUNK_BGN_FUNC_FAIL    1 /* tell the lib to end the task */\n#define CURL_CHUNK_BGN_FUNC_SKIP    2 /* skip this chunk over */\n\n/* if splitting of data transfer is enabled, this callback is called before\n   download of an individual chunk started. Note that parameter \"remains\" works\n   only for FTP wildcard downloading (for now), otherwise is not used */\ntypedef long (*curl_chunk_bgn_callback)(const void *transfer_info,\n                                        void *ptr,\n                                        int remains);\n\n/* return codes for CURLOPT_CHUNK_END_FUNCTION */\n#define CURL_CHUNK_END_FUNC_OK      0\n#define CURL_CHUNK_END_FUNC_FAIL    1 /* tell the lib to end the task */\n\n/* If splitting of data transfer is enabled this callback is called after\n   download of an individual chunk finished.\n   Note! After this callback was set then it have to be called FOR ALL chunks.\n   Even if downloading of this chunk was skipped in CHUNK_BGN_FUNC.\n   This is the reason why we do not need \"transfer_info\" parameter in this\n   callback and we are not interested in \"remains\" parameter too. */\ntypedef long (*curl_chunk_end_callback)(void *ptr);\n\n/* return codes for FNMATCHFUNCTION */\n#define CURL_FNMATCHFUNC_MATCH    0 /* string corresponds to the pattern */\n#define CURL_FNMATCHFUNC_NOMATCH  1 /* pattern does not match the string */\n#define CURL_FNMATCHFUNC_FAIL     2 /* an error occurred */\n\n/* callback type for wildcard downloading pattern matching. If the\n   string matches the pattern, return CURL_FNMATCHFUNC_MATCH value, etc. */\ntypedef int (*curl_fnmatch_callback)(void *ptr,\n                                     const char *pattern,\n                                     const char *string);\n\n/* These are the return codes for the seek callbacks */\n#define CURL_SEEKFUNC_OK       0\n#define CURL_SEEKFUNC_FAIL     1 /* fail the entire transfer */\n#define CURL_SEEKFUNC_CANTSEEK 2 /* tell libcurl seeking cannot be done, so\n                                    libcurl might try other means instead */\ntypedef int (*curl_seek_callback)(void *instream,\n                                  curl_off_t offset,\n                                  int origin); /* 'whence' */\n\n/* This is a return code for the read callback that, when returned, will\n   signal libcurl to immediately abort the current transfer. */\n#define CURL_READFUNC_ABORT 0x10000000\n/* This is a return code for the read callback that, when returned, will\n   signal libcurl to pause sending data on the current transfer. */\n#define CURL_READFUNC_PAUSE 0x10000001\n\n/* Return code for when the trailing headers' callback has terminated\n   without any errors */\n#define CURL_TRAILERFUNC_OK 0\n/* Return code for when was an error in the trailing header's list and we\n  want to abort the request */\n#define CURL_TRAILERFUNC_ABORT 1\n\ntypedef size_t (*curl_read_callback)(char *buffer,\n                                     size_t size,\n                                     size_t nitems,\n                                     void *instream);\n\ntypedef int (*curl_trailer_callback)(struct curl_slist **list,\n                                     void *userdata);\n\ntypedef enum {\n  CURLSOCKTYPE_IPCXN,  /* socket created for a specific IP connection */\n  CURLSOCKTYPE_ACCEPT, /* socket created by accept() call */\n  CURLSOCKTYPE_LAST    /* never use */\n} curlsocktype;\n\n/* The return code from the sockopt_callback can signal information back\n   to libcurl: */\n#define CURL_SOCKOPT_OK 0\n#define CURL_SOCKOPT_ERROR 1 /* causes libcurl to abort and return\n                                CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK */\n#define CURL_SOCKOPT_ALREADY_CONNECTED 2\n\ntypedef int (*curl_sockopt_callback)(void *clientp,\n                                     curl_socket_t curlfd,\n                                     curlsocktype purpose);\n\nstruct curl_sockaddr {\n  int family;\n  int socktype;\n  int protocol;\n  unsigned int addrlen; /* addrlen was a socklen_t type before 7.18.0 but it\n                           turned really ugly and painful on the systems that\n                           lack this type */\n  struct sockaddr addr;\n};\n\ntypedef curl_socket_t\n(*curl_opensocket_callback)(void *clientp,\n                            curlsocktype purpose,\n                            struct curl_sockaddr *address);\n\ntypedef int\n(*curl_closesocket_callback)(void *clientp, curl_socket_t item);\n\ntypedef enum {\n  CURLIOE_OK,            /* I/O operation successful */\n  CURLIOE_UNKNOWNCMD,    /* command was unknown to callback */\n  CURLIOE_FAILRESTART,   /* failed to restart the read */\n  CURLIOE_LAST           /* never use */\n} curlioerr;\n\ntypedef enum {\n  CURLIOCMD_NOP,         /* no operation */\n  CURLIOCMD_RESTARTREAD, /* restart the read stream from start */\n  CURLIOCMD_LAST         /* never use */\n} curliocmd;\n\ntypedef curlioerr (*curl_ioctl_callback)(CURL *handle,\n                                         int cmd,\n                                         void *clientp);\n\n#ifndef CURL_DID_MEMORY_FUNC_TYPEDEFS\n/*\n * The following typedef's are signatures of malloc, free, realloc, strdup and\n * calloc respectively. Function pointers of these types can be passed to the\n * curl_global_init_mem() function to set user defined memory management\n * callback routines.\n */\ntypedef void *(*curl_malloc_callback)(size_t size);\ntypedef void (*curl_free_callback)(void *ptr);\ntypedef void *(*curl_realloc_callback)(void *ptr, size_t size);\ntypedef char *(*curl_strdup_callback)(const char *str);\ntypedef void *(*curl_calloc_callback)(size_t nmemb, size_t size);\n\n#define CURL_DID_MEMORY_FUNC_TYPEDEFS\n#endif\n\n/* the kind of data that is passed to information_callback */\ntypedef enum {\n  CURLINFO_TEXT = 0,\n  CURLINFO_HEADER_IN,    /* 1 */\n  CURLINFO_HEADER_OUT,   /* 2 */\n  CURLINFO_DATA_IN,      /* 3 */\n  CURLINFO_DATA_OUT,     /* 4 */\n  CURLINFO_SSL_DATA_IN,  /* 5 */\n  CURLINFO_SSL_DATA_OUT, /* 6 */\n  CURLINFO_END\n} curl_infotype;\n\ntypedef int (*curl_debug_callback)\n       (CURL *handle,      /* the handle/transfer this concerns */\n        curl_infotype type, /* what kind of data */\n        char *data,        /* points to the data */\n        size_t size,       /* size of the data pointed to */\n        void *userptr);    /* whatever the user please */\n\n/* This is the CURLOPT_PREREQFUNCTION callback prototype. */\ntypedef int (*curl_prereq_callback)(void *clientp,\n                                    char *conn_primary_ip,\n                                    char *conn_local_ip,\n                                    int conn_primary_port,\n                                    int conn_local_port);\n\n/* Return code for when the pre-request callback has terminated without\n   any errors */\n#define CURL_PREREQFUNC_OK 0\n/* Return code for when the pre-request callback wants to abort the\n   request */\n#define CURL_PREREQFUNC_ABORT 1\n\n/* All possible error codes from all sorts of curl functions. Future versions\n   may return other values, stay prepared.\n\n   Always add new return codes last. Never *EVER* remove any. The return\n   codes must remain the same!\n */\n\ntypedef enum {\n  CURLE_OK = 0,\n  CURLE_UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL,    /* 1 */\n  CURLE_FAILED_INIT,             /* 2 */\n  CURLE_URL_MALFORMAT,           /* 3 */\n  CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN,            /* 4 - [was obsoleted in August 2007 for\n                                    7.17.0, reused in April 2011 for 7.21.5] */\n  CURLE_COULDNT_RESOLVE_PROXY,   /* 5 */\n  CURLE_COULDNT_RESOLVE_HOST,    /* 6 */\n  CURLE_COULDNT_CONNECT,         /* 7 */\n  CURLE_WEIRD_SERVER_REPLY,      /* 8 */\n  CURLE_REMOTE_ACCESS_DENIED,    /* 9 a service was denied by the server\n                                    due to lack of access - when login fails\n                                    this is not returned. */\n  CURLE_FTP_ACCEPT_FAILED,       /* 10 - [was obsoleted in April 2006 for\n                                    7.15.4, reused in Dec 2011 for 7.24.0]*/\n  CURLE_FTP_WEIRD_PASS_REPLY,    /* 11 */\n  CURLE_FTP_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT,      /* 12 - timeout occurred accepting server\n                                    [was obsoleted in August 2007 for 7.17.0,\n                                    reused in Dec 2011 for 7.24.0]*/\n  CURLE_FTP_WEIRD_PASV_REPLY,    /* 13 */\n  CURLE_FTP_WEIRD_227_FORMAT,    /* 14 */\n  CURLE_FTP_CANT_GET_HOST,       /* 15 */\n  CURLE_HTTP2,                   /* 16 - A problem in the http2 framing layer.\n                                    [was obsoleted in August 2007 for 7.17.0,\n                                    reused in July 2014 for 7.38.0] */\n  CURLE_FTP_COULDNT_SET_TYPE,    /* 17 */\n  CURLE_PARTIAL_FILE,            /* 18 */\n  CURLE_FTP_COULDNT_RETR_FILE,   /* 19 */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE20,              /* 20 - NOT USED */\n  CURLE_QUOTE_ERROR,             /* 21 - quote command failure */\n  CURLE_HTTP_RETURNED_ERROR,     /* 22 */\n  CURLE_WRITE_ERROR,             /* 23 */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE24,              /* 24 - NOT USED */\n  CURLE_UPLOAD_FAILED,           /* 25 - failed upload \"command\" */\n  CURLE_READ_ERROR,              /* 26 - could not open/read from file */\n  CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY,           /* 27 */\n  CURLE_OPERATION_TIMEDOUT,      /* 28 - the timeout time was reached */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE29,              /* 29 - NOT USED */\n  CURLE_FTP_PORT_FAILED,         /* 30 - FTP PORT operation failed */\n  CURLE_FTP_COULDNT_USE_REST,    /* 31 - the REST command failed */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE32,              /* 32 - NOT USED */\n  CURLE_RANGE_ERROR,             /* 33 - RANGE \"command\" did not work */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE34,              /* 34 */\n  CURLE_SSL_CONNECT_ERROR,       /* 35 - wrong when connecting with SSL */\n  CURLE_BAD_DOWNLOAD_RESUME,     /* 36 - could not resume download */\n  CURLE_FILE_COULDNT_READ_FILE,  /* 37 */\n  CURLE_LDAP_CANNOT_BIND,        /* 38 */\n  CURLE_LDAP_SEARCH_FAILED,      /* 39 */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE40,              /* 40 - NOT USED */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE41,              /* 41 - NOT USED starting with 7.53.0 */\n  CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK,     /* 42 */\n  CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT,   /* 43 */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE44,              /* 44 - NOT USED */\n  CURLE_INTERFACE_FAILED,        /* 45 - CURLOPT_INTERFACE failed */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE46,              /* 46 - NOT USED */\n  CURLE_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS,      /* 47 - catch endless re-direct loops */\n  CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION,          /* 48 - User specified an unknown option */\n  CURLE_SETOPT_OPTION_SYNTAX,    /* 49 - Malformed setopt option */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE50,              /* 50 - NOT USED */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE51,              /* 51 - NOT USED */\n  CURLE_GOT_NOTHING,             /* 52 - when this is a specific error */\n  CURLE_SSL_ENGINE_NOTFOUND,     /* 53 - SSL crypto engine not found */\n  CURLE_SSL_ENGINE_SETFAILED,    /* 54 - can not set SSL crypto engine as\n                                    default */\n  CURLE_SEND_ERROR,              /* 55 - failed sending network data */\n  CURLE_RECV_ERROR,              /* 56 - failure in receiving network data */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE57,              /* 57 - NOT IN USE */\n  CURLE_SSL_CERTPROBLEM,         /* 58 - problem with the local certificate */\n  CURLE_SSL_CIPHER,              /* 59 - could not use specified cipher */\n  CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION, /* 60 - peer's certificate or fingerprint\n                                     was not verified fine */\n  CURLE_BAD_CONTENT_ENCODING,    /* 61 - Unrecognized/bad encoding */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE62,              /* 62 - NOT IN USE since 7.82.0 */\n  CURLE_FILESIZE_EXCEEDED,       /* 63 - Maximum file size exceeded */\n  CURLE_USE_SSL_FAILED,          /* 64 - Requested FTP SSL level failed */\n  CURLE_SEND_FAIL_REWIND,        /* 65 - Sending the data requires a rewind\n                                    that failed */\n  CURLE_SSL_ENGINE_INITFAILED,   /* 66 - failed to initialise ENGINE */\n  CURLE_LOGIN_DENIED,            /* 67 - user, password or similar was not\n                                    accepted and we failed to login */\n  CURLE_TFTP_NOTFOUND,           /* 68 - file not found on server */\n  CURLE_TFTP_PERM,               /* 69 - permission problem on server */\n  CURLE_REMOTE_DISK_FULL,        /* 70 - out of disk space on server */\n  CURLE_TFTP_ILLEGAL,            /* 71 - Illegal TFTP operation */\n  CURLE_TFTP_UNKNOWNID,          /* 72 - Unknown transfer ID */\n  CURLE_REMOTE_FILE_EXISTS,      /* 73 - File already exists */\n  CURLE_TFTP_NOSUCHUSER,         /* 74 - No such user */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE75,              /* 75 - NOT IN USE since 7.82.0 */\n  CURLE_OBSOLETE76,              /* 76 - NOT IN USE since 7.82.0 */\n  CURLE_SSL_CACERT_BADFILE,      /* 77 - could not load CACERT file, missing\n                                    or wrong format */\n  CURLE_REMOTE_FILE_NOT_FOUND,   /* 78 - remote file not found */\n  CURLE_SSH,                     /* 79 - error from the SSH layer, somewhat\n                                    generic so the error message will be of\n                                    interest when this has happened */\n\n  CURLE_SSL_SHUTDOWN_FAILED,     /* 80 - Failed to shut down the SSL\n                                    connection */\n  CURLE_AGAIN,                   /* 81 - socket is not ready for send/recv,\n                                    wait till it is ready and try again (Added\n                                    in 7.18.2) */\n  CURLE_SSL_CRL_BADFILE,         /* 82 - could not load CRL file, missing or\n                                    wrong format (Added in 7.19.0) */\n  CURLE_SSL_ISSUER_ERROR,        /* 83 - Issuer check failed.  (Added in\n                                    7.19.0) */\n  CURLE_FTP_PRET_FAILED,         /* 84 - a PRET command failed */\n  CURLE_RTSP_CSEQ_ERROR,         /* 85 - mismatch of RTSP CSeq numbers */\n  CURLE_RTSP_SESSION_ERROR,      /* 86 - mismatch of RTSP Session Ids */\n  CURLE_FTP_BAD_FILE_LIST,       /* 87 - unable to parse FTP file list */\n  CURLE_CHUNK_FAILED,            /* 88 - chunk callback reported error */\n  CURLE_NO_CONNECTION_AVAILABLE, /* 89 - No connection available, the\n                                    session will be queued */\n  CURLE_SSL_PINNEDPUBKEYNOTMATCH, /* 90 - specified pinned public key did not\n                                     match */\n  CURLE_SSL_INVALIDCERTSTATUS,   /* 91 - invalid certificate status */\n  CURLE_HTTP2_STREAM,            /* 92 - stream error in HTTP/2 framing layer\n                                    */\n  CURLE_RECURSIVE_API_CALL,      /* 93 - an api function was called from\n                                    inside a callback */\n  CURLE_AUTH_ERROR,              /* 94 - an authentication function returned an\n                                    error */\n  CURLE_HTTP3,                   /* 95 - An HTTP/3 layer problem */\n  CURLE_QUIC_CONNECT_ERROR,      /* 96 - QUIC connection error */\n  CURLE_PROXY,                   /* 97 - proxy handshake error */\n  CURLE_SSL_CLIENTCERT,          /* 98 - client-side certificate required */\n  CURLE_UNRECOVERABLE_POLL,      /* 99 - poll/select returned fatal error */\n  CURLE_TOO_LARGE,               /* 100 - a value/data met its maximum */\n  CURLE_ECH_REQUIRED,            /* 101 - ECH tried but failed */\n  CURL_LAST /* never use! */\n} CURLcode;\n\n#ifndef CURL_NO_OLDIES /* define this to test if your app builds with all\n                          the obsolete stuff removed! */\n\n/* removed in 7.53.0 */\n#define CURLE_FUNCTION_NOT_FOUND CURLE_OBSOLETE41\n\n/* removed in 7.56.0 */\n#define CURLE_HTTP_POST_ERROR CURLE_OBSOLETE34\n\n/* Previously obsolete error code reused in 7.38.0 */\n#define CURLE_OBSOLETE16 CURLE_HTTP2\n\n/* Previously obsolete error codes reused in 7.24.0 */\n#define CURLE_OBSOLETE10 CURLE_FTP_ACCEPT_FAILED\n#define CURLE_OBSOLETE12 CURLE_FTP_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT\n\n/*  compatibility with older names */\n#define CURLOPT_ENCODING CURLOPT_ACCEPT_ENCODING\n#define CURLE_FTP_WEIRD_SERVER_REPLY CURLE_WEIRD_SERVER_REPLY\n\n/* The following were added in 7.62.0 */\n#define CURLE_SSL_CACERT CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION\n\n/* The following were added in 7.21.5, April 2011 */\n#define CURLE_UNKNOWN_TELNET_OPTION CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION\n\n/* Added for 7.78.0 */\n#define CURLE_TELNET_OPTION_SYNTAX CURLE_SETOPT_OPTION_SYNTAX\n\n/* The following were added in 7.17.1 */\n/* These are scheduled to disappear by 2009 */\n#define CURLE_SSL_PEER_CERTIFICATE CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION\n\n/* The following were added in 7.17.0 */\n/* These are scheduled to disappear by 2009 */\n#define CURLE_OBSOLETE CURLE_OBSOLETE50 /* no one should be using this! */\n#define CURLE_BAD_PASSWORD_ENTERED CURLE_OBSOLETE46\n#define CURLE_BAD_CALLING_ORDER CURLE_OBSOLETE44\n#define CURLE_FTP_USER_PASSWORD_INCORRECT CURLE_OBSOLETE10\n#define CURLE_FTP_CANT_RECONNECT CURLE_OBSOLETE16\n#define CURLE_FTP_COULDNT_GET_SIZE CURLE_OBSOLETE32\n#define CURLE_FTP_COULDNT_SET_ASCII CURLE_OBSOLETE29\n#define CURLE_FTP_WEIRD_USER_REPLY CURLE_OBSOLETE12\n#define CURLE_FTP_WRITE_ERROR CURLE_OBSOLETE20\n#define CURLE_LIBRARY_NOT_FOUND CURLE_OBSOLETE40\n#define CURLE_MALFORMAT_USER CURLE_OBSOLETE24\n#define CURLE_SHARE_IN_USE CURLE_OBSOLETE57\n#define CURLE_URL_MALFORMAT_USER CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN\n\n#define CURLE_FTP_ACCESS_DENIED CURLE_REMOTE_ACCESS_DENIED\n#define CURLE_FTP_COULDNT_SET_BINARY CURLE_FTP_COULDNT_SET_TYPE\n#define CURLE_FTP_QUOTE_ERROR CURLE_QUOTE_ERROR\n#define CURLE_TFTP_DISKFULL CURLE_REMOTE_DISK_FULL\n#define CURLE_TFTP_EXISTS CURLE_REMOTE_FILE_EXISTS\n#define CURLE_HTTP_RANGE_ERROR CURLE_RANGE_ERROR\n#define CURLE_FTP_SSL_FAILED CURLE_USE_SSL_FAILED\n\n/* The following were added earlier */\n\n#define CURLE_OPERATION_TIMEOUTED CURLE_OPERATION_TIMEDOUT\n#define CURLE_HTTP_NOT_FOUND CURLE_HTTP_RETURNED_ERROR\n#define CURLE_HTTP_PORT_FAILED CURLE_INTERFACE_FAILED\n#define CURLE_FTP_COULDNT_STOR_FILE CURLE_UPLOAD_FAILED\n#define CURLE_FTP_PARTIAL_FILE CURLE_PARTIAL_FILE\n#define CURLE_FTP_BAD_DOWNLOAD_RESUME CURLE_BAD_DOWNLOAD_RESUME\n#define CURLE_LDAP_INVALID_URL CURLE_OBSOLETE62\n#define CURLE_CONV_REQD CURLE_OBSOLETE76\n#define CURLE_CONV_FAILED CURLE_OBSOLETE75\n\n/* This was the error code 50 in 7.7.3 and a few earlier versions, this\n   is no longer used by libcurl but is instead #defined here only to not\n   make programs break */\n#define CURLE_ALREADY_COMPLETE 99999\n\n/* Provide defines for really old option names */\n#define CURLOPT_FILE CURLOPT_WRITEDATA /* name changed in 7.9.7 */\n#define CURLOPT_INFILE CURLOPT_READDATA /* name changed in 7.9.7 */\n#define CURLOPT_WRITEHEADER CURLOPT_HEADERDATA\n\n/* Since long deprecated options with no code in the lib that does anything\n   with them. */\n#define CURLOPT_WRITEINFO CURLOPT_OBSOLETE40\n#define CURLOPT_CLOSEPOLICY CURLOPT_OBSOLETE72\n#define CURLOPT_OBSOLETE72 9999\n#define CURLOPT_OBSOLETE40 9999\n\n#endif /* !CURL_NO_OLDIES */\n\n/*\n * Proxy error codes. Returned in CURLINFO_PROXY_ERROR if CURLE_PROXY was\n * return for the transfers.\n */\ntypedef enum {\n  CURLPX_OK,\n  CURLPX_BAD_ADDRESS_TYPE,\n  CURLPX_BAD_VERSION,\n  CURLPX_CLOSED,\n  CURLPX_GSSAPI,\n  CURLPX_GSSAPI_PERMSG,\n  CURLPX_GSSAPI_PROTECTION,\n  CURLPX_IDENTD,\n  CURLPX_IDENTD_DIFFER,\n  CURLPX_LONG_HOSTNAME,\n  CURLPX_LONG_PASSWD,\n  CURLPX_LONG_USER,\n  CURLPX_NO_AUTH,\n  CURLPX_RECV_ADDRESS,\n  CURLPX_RECV_AUTH,\n  CURLPX_RECV_CONNECT,\n  CURLPX_RECV_REQACK,\n  CURLPX_REPLY_ADDRESS_TYPE_NOT_SUPPORTED,\n  CURLPX_REPLY_COMMAND_NOT_SUPPORTED,\n  CURLPX_REPLY_CONNECTION_REFUSED,\n  CURLPX_REPLY_GENERAL_SERVER_FAILURE,\n  CURLPX_REPLY_HOST_UNREACHABLE,\n  CURLPX_REPLY_NETWORK_UNREACHABLE,\n  CURLPX_REPLY_NOT_ALLOWED,\n  CURLPX_REPLY_TTL_EXPIRED,\n  CURLPX_REPLY_UNASSIGNED,\n  CURLPX_REQUEST_FAILED,\n  CURLPX_RESOLVE_HOST,\n  CURLPX_SEND_AUTH,\n  CURLPX_SEND_CONNECT,\n  CURLPX_SEND_REQUEST,\n  CURLPX_UNKNOWN_FAIL,\n  CURLPX_UNKNOWN_MODE,\n  CURLPX_USER_REJECTED,\n  CURLPX_LAST /* never use */\n} CURLproxycode;\n\n/* This prototype applies to all conversion callbacks */\ntypedef CURLcode (*curl_conv_callback)(char *buffer, size_t length);\n\ntypedef CURLcode (*curl_ssl_ctx_callback)(CURL *curl,    /* easy handle */\n                                          void *ssl_ctx, /* actually an OpenSSL\n                                                            or wolfSSL SSL_CTX,\n                                                            or an mbedTLS\n                                                          mbedtls_ssl_config */\n                                          void *userptr);\n\n#define CURLPROXY_HTTP            0L /* added in 7.10, new in 7.19.4 default is\n                                        to use CONNECT HTTP/1.1 */\n#define CURLPROXY_HTTP_1_0        1L /* force to use CONNECT HTTP/1.0\n                                        added in 7.19.4 */\n#define CURLPROXY_HTTPS           2L /* HTTPS but stick to HTTP/1\n                                        added in 7.52.0 */\n#define CURLPROXY_HTTPS2          3L /* HTTPS and attempt HTTP/2\n                                        added in 8.2.0 */\n#define CURLPROXY_SOCKS4          4L /* support added in 7.15.2, enum existed\n                                        already in 7.10 */\n#define CURLPROXY_SOCKS5          5L /* added in 7.10 */\n#define CURLPROXY_SOCKS4A         6L /* added in 7.18.0 */\n#define CURLPROXY_SOCKS5_HOSTNAME 7L /* Use the SOCKS5 protocol but pass along\n                                        the hostname rather than the IP\n                                        address. added in 7.18.0 */\n\ntypedef enum {\n  CURLPROXY_LAST = 8 /* never use */\n} curl_proxytype;  /* this enum was added
