\"curl_trc.h\"\n#include \"rand.h\"\n#include \"multiif.h\"\n#include \"cfilters.h\"\n#include \"cf-socket.h\"\n#include \"connect.h\"\n#include \"progress.h\"\n#include \"curlx/fopen.h\"\n#include \"curlx/dynbuf.h\"\n#include \"http1.h\"\n#include \"select.h\"\n#include \"transfer.h\"\n#include \"bufref.h\"\n#include \"vquic/vquic.h\"\n#include \"vquic/vquic_int.h\"\n#include \"vquic/vquic-tls.h\"\n#include \"vtls/keylog.h\"\n#include \"vtls/vtls.h\"\n#include \"vtls/vtls_scache.h\"\n#include \"vquic/curl_ngtcp2.h\"\n\n\n#define QUIC_MAX_STREAMS       (256 * 1024)\n#define QUIC_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT (10 * NGTCP2_SECONDS)\n\n/* We announce a small window size in transport param to the server,\n * and grow that immediately to max when no rate limit is in place.\n * We need to start small as we are not able to decrease it. */\n#define H3_STREAM_WINDOW_SIZE_INITIAL (32 * 1024)\n#define H3_STREAM_WINDOW_SIZE_MAX     (10 * 1024 * 1024)\n#define H3_CONN_WINDOW_SIZE_MAX       (100 * H3_STREAM_WINDOW_SIZE_MAX)\n\n#define H3_STREAM_CHUNK_SIZE  (64 * 1024)\n#if H3_STREAM_CHUNK_SIZE < NGTCP2_MAX_UDP_PAYLOAD_SIZE\n#error H3_STREAM_CHUNK_SIZE smaller than NGTCP2_MAX_UDP_PAYLOAD_SIZE\n#endif\n\n/* The pool keeps spares around and half of a full stream windows\n * seems good. More does not seem to improve performance.\n * The benefit of the pool is that stream buffer to not keep\n * spares. Memory consumption goes down when streams run empty,\n * have a large upload done, etc. */\n#define H3_STREAM_POOL_SPARES      2\n/* The max amount of un-acked upload data we keep around per stream */\n#define H3_STREAM_SEND_BUFFER_MAX      (10 * 1024 * 1024)\n#define H3_STREAM_SEND_CHUNKS \\\n  (H3_STREAM_SEND_BUFFER_MAX / H3_STREAM_CHUNK_SIZE)\n\n/*\n * Store ngtcp2 version info in this buffer.\n */\nvoid Curl_ngtcp2_ver(char *p, size_t len)\n{\n  const ngtcp2_info *ng2 = ngtcp2_version(0);\n  const nghttp3_info *ht3 = nghttp3_version(0);\n  (void)curl_msnprintf(p, len, \"ngtcp2/%s nghttp3/%s\",\n                       ng2->version_str, ht3->version_str);\n}\n\nstruct cf_ngtcp2_ctx {\n  struct cf_quic_ctx q;\n  struct ssl_peer peer;\n  struct curl_tls_ctx tls;\n#ifdef OPENSSL_QUIC_API2\n  ngtcp2_crypto_ossl_ctx *ossl_ctx;\n#endif\n  ngtcp2_path connected_path;\n  ngtcp2_conn *qconn;\n  ngtcp2_cid dcid;\n  ngtcp2_cid scid;\n  uint32_t version;\n  ngtcp2_settings settings;\n  ngtcp2_transport_params transport_params;\n  ngtcp2_ccerr last_error;\n  ngtcp2_crypto_conn_ref conn_ref;\n  struct cf_call_data call_data;\n  nghttp3_conn *h3conn;\n  nghttp3_settings h3settings;\n  struct curltime started_at;        /* time the current attempt started */\n  struct curltime handshake_at;      /* time connect handshake finished */\n  struct bufc_pool stream_bufcp;     /* chunk pool for streams */\n  struct dynbuf scratch;             /* temp buffer for header construction */\n  struct uint_hash streams;          /* hash `data->mid` to `h3_stream_ctx` */\n  uint64_t used_bidi_streams;        /* bidi streams we have opened */\n  uint64_t max_bidi_streams;         /* max bidi streams we can open */\n  size_t earlydata_max;              /* max amount of early data supported by\n                                        server on session reuse */\n  size_t earlydata_skip;             /* sending bytes to skip when earlydata\n                                        is accepted by peer */\n  CURLcode tls_vrfy_result;          /* result of TLS peer verification */\n  int qlogfd;\n  BIT(initialized);\n  BIT(tls_handshake_complete);       /* TLS handshake is done */\n  BIT(use_earlydata);                /* Using 0RTT data */\n  BIT(earlydata_accepted);           /* 0RTT was accepted by server */\n  BIT(shutdown_started);             /* queued shutdown packets */\n};\n\n/* How to access `call_data` from a cf_ngtcp2 filter */\n#undef CF_CTX_CALL_DATA\n#define CF_CTX_CALL_DATA(cf) ((struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *)(cf)->ctx)->call_data\n\nstatic void h3_stream_hash_free(unsigned int id, void *stream);\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_ctx_init(struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx)\n{\n  DEBUGASSERT(!ctx->initialized);\n  ctx->qlogfd = -1;\n  ctx->version = NGTCP2_PROTO_VER_MAX;\n  Curl_bufcp_init(&ctx->stream_bufcp, H3_STREAM_CHUNK_SIZE,\n                  H3_STREAM_POOL_SPARES);\n  curlx_dyn_init(&ctx->scratch, CURL_MAX_HTTP_HEADER);\n  Curl_uint32_hash_init(&ctx->streams, 63, h3_stream_hash_free);\n  ctx->initialized = TRUE;\n}\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_ctx_free(struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx)\n{\n  if(ctx && ctx->initialized) {\n    Curl_vquic_tls_cleanup(&ctx->tls);\n    vquic_ctx_free(&ctx->q);\n    Curl_bufcp_free(&ctx->stream_bufcp);\n    curlx_dyn_free(&ctx->scratch);\n    Curl_uint32_hash_destroy(&ctx->streams);\n    Curl_ssl_peer_cleanup(&ctx->peer);\n  }\n  curlx_free(ctx);\n}\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_setup_keep_alive(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                       struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  const ngtcp2_transport_params *rp;\n  /* Peer should have sent us its transport parameters. If it\n   * announces a positive `max_idle_timeout` it will close the\n   * connection when it does not hear from us for that time.\n   *\n   * Some servers use this as a keep-alive timer at a rather low\n   * value. We are doing HTTP/3 here and waiting for the response\n   * to a request may take a considerable amount of time. We need\n   * to prevent the peer's QUIC stack from closing in this case.\n   */\n  if(!ctx->qconn)\n    return;\n\n  rp = ngtcp2_conn_get_remote_transport_params(ctx->qconn);\n  if(!rp || !rp->max_idle_timeout) {\n    ngtcp2_conn_set_keep_alive_timeout(ctx->qconn, UINT64_MAX);\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"no peer idle timeout, unset keep-alive\");\n  }\n  else if(!Curl_uint32_hash_count(&ctx->streams)) {\n    ngtcp2_conn_set_keep_alive_timeout(ctx->qconn, UINT64_MAX);\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"no active streams, unset keep-alive\");\n  }\n  else {\n    ngtcp2_duration keep_ns;\n    keep_ns = (rp->max_idle_timeout > 1) ? (rp->max_idle_timeout / 2) : 1;\n    ngtcp2_conn_set_keep_alive_timeout(ctx->qconn, keep_ns);\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"peer idle timeout is %\" PRIu64 \"ms, \"\n                \"set keep-alive to %\" PRIu64 \" ms.\",\n                (rp->max_idle_timeout / NGTCP2_MILLISECONDS),\n                (keep_ns / NGTCP2_MILLISECONDS));\n  }\n}\n\nstruct pkt_io_ctx;\nstatic CURLcode cf_progress_ingress(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                    struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                    struct pkt_io_ctx *pktx);\nstatic CURLcode cf_progress_egress(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                   struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                   struct pkt_io_ctx *pktx);\n\n/**\n * All about the H3 internals of a stream\n */\nstruct h3_stream_ctx {\n  int64_t id;                   /* HTTP/3 protocol identifier */\n  struct bufq sendbuf;          /* h3 request body */\n  struct h1_req_parser h1;      /* h1 request parsing */\n  size_t sendbuf_len_in_flight; /* sendbuf amount \"in flight\" */\n  uint64_t error3;              /* HTTP/3 stream error code */\n  curl_off_t upload_left;       /* number of request bytes left to upload */\n  uint64_t rx_offset;           /* current receive offset */\n  uint64_t rx_offset_max;       /* allowed receive offset */\n  uint64_t window_size_max;     /* max flow control window set for stream */\n  int status_code;              /* HTTP status code */\n  CURLcode xfer_result;         /* result from xfer_resp_write(_hd) */\n  BIT(resp_hds_complete);       /* we have a complete, final response */\n  BIT(closed);                  /* TRUE on stream close */\n  BIT(reset);                   /* TRUE on stream reset */\n  BIT(send_closed);             /* stream is local closed */\n  BIT(quic_flow_blocked);       /* stream is blocked by QUIC flow control */\n};\n\nstatic void h3_stream_ctx_free(struct h3_stream_ctx *stream)\n{\n  Curl_bufq_free(&stream->sendbuf);\n  Curl_h1_req_parse_free(&stream->h1);\n  curlx_free(stream);\n}\n\nstatic void h3_stream_hash_free(unsigned int id, void *stream)\n{\n  (void)id;\n  DEBUGASSERT(stream);\n  h3_stream_ctx_free((struct h3_stream_ctx *)stream);\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode h3_data_setup(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                              struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n\n  if(!data)\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n\n  if(stream)\n    return CURLE_OK;\n\n  stream = curlx_calloc(1, sizeof(*stream));\n  if(!stream)\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n\n  stream->id = -1;\n  stream->rx_offset = 0;\n  stream->rx_offset_max = H3_STREAM_WINDOW_SIZE_INITIAL;\n\n  /* on send, we control how much we put into the buffer */\n  Curl_bufq_initp(&stream->sendbuf, &ctx->stream_bufcp,\n                  H3_STREAM_SEND_CHUNKS, BUFQ_OPT_NONE);\n  stream->sendbuf_len_in_flight = 0;\n  stream->window_size_max = H3_STREAM_WINDOW_SIZE_INITIAL;\n  Curl_h1_req_parse_init(&stream->h1, H1_PARSE_DEFAULT_MAX_LINE_LEN);\n\n  if(!Curl_uint32_hash_set(&ctx->streams, data->mid, stream)) {\n    h3_stream_ctx_free(stream);\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n  }\n\n  if(Curl_uint32_hash_count(&ctx->streams) == 1)\n    cf_ngtcp2_setup_keep_alive(cf, data);\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\n#if NGTCP2_VERSION_NUM < 0x011100\nstruct cf_ngtcp2_sfind_ctx {\n  int64_t stream_id;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream;\n  uint32_t mid;\n};\n\nstatic bool cf_ngtcp2_sfind(uint32_t mid, void *value, void *user_data)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_sfind_ctx *fctx = user_data;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = value;\n\n  if(fctx->stream_id == stream->id) {\n    fctx->mid = mid;\n    fctx->stream = stream;\n    return FALSE;\n  }\n  return TRUE; /* continue */\n}\n\nstatic struct h3_stream_ctx *cf_ngtcp2_get_stream(struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx,\n                                                  int64_t stream_id)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_sfind_ctx fctx;\n  fctx.stream_id = stream_id;\n  fctx.stream = NULL;\n  Curl_uint32_hash_visit(&ctx->streams, cf_ngtcp2_sfind, &fctx);\n  return fctx.stream;\n}\n#else\nstatic struct h3_stream_ctx *cf_ngtcp2_get_stream(struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx,\n                                                  int64_t stream_id)\n{\n  struct Curl_easy *data =\n    ngtcp2_conn_get_stream_user_data(ctx->qconn, stream_id);\n\n  if(!data) {\n    return NULL;\n  }\n\n  return H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n}\n#endif\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_stream_close(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                   struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                   struct h3_stream_ctx *stream)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  DEBUGASSERT(data);\n  DEBUGASSERT(stream);\n  if(!stream->closed && ctx->qconn && ctx->h3conn) {\n    CURLcode result;\n\n    nghttp3_conn_set_stream_user_data(ctx->h3conn, stream->id, NULL);\n    ngtcp2_conn_set_stream_user_data(ctx->qconn, stream->id, NULL);\n    stream->closed = TRUE;\n    (void)ngtcp2_conn_shutdown_stream(ctx->qconn, 0, stream->id,\n                                      NGHTTP3_H3_REQUEST_CANCELLED);\n    result = cf_progress_egress(cf, data, NULL);\n    if(result)\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] cancel stream -> %d\",\n                  stream->id, result);\n  }\n}\n\nstatic void h3_data_done(struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n  (void)cf;\n  if(stream) {\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] easy handle is done\", stream->id);\n    cf_ngtcp2_stream_close(cf, data, stream);\n    Curl_uint32_hash_remove(&ctx->streams, data->mid);\n    if(!Curl_uint32_hash_count(&ctx->streams))\n      cf_ngtcp2_setup_keep_alive(cf, data);\n  }\n}\n\nstruct pkt_io_ctx {\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf;\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n  ngtcp2_tstamp ts;\n  ngtcp2_path_storage ps;\n};\n\nstatic void pktx_update_time(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                             struct pkt_io_ctx *pktx,\n                             struct Curl_cfilter *cf)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  const struct curltime *pnow = Curl_pgrs_now(data);\n\n  vquic_ctx_update_time(&ctx->q, pnow);\n  pktx->ts = ((ngtcp2_tstamp)pnow->tv_sec * NGTCP2_SECONDS) +\n             ((ngtcp2_tstamp)pnow->tv_usec * NGTCP2_MICROSECONDS);\n}\n\nstatic void pktx_init(struct pkt_io_ctx *pktx,\n                      struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                      struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  const struct curltime *pnow = Curl_pgrs_now(data);\n\n  pktx->cf = cf;\n  pktx->data = data;\n  ngtcp2_path_storage_zero(&pktx->ps);\n  vquic_ctx_set_time(&ctx->q, pnow);\n  pktx->ts = ((ngtcp2_tstamp)pnow->tv_sec * NGTCP2_SECONDS) +\n             ((ngtcp2_tstamp)pnow->tv_usec * NGTCP2_MICROSECONDS);\n}\n\nstatic int cb_h3_acked_req_body(nghttp3_conn *conn, int64_t stream_id,\n                                uint64_t datalen, void *user_data,\n                                void *stream_user_data);\n\nstatic ngtcp2_conn *get_conn(ngtcp2_crypto_conn_ref *conn_ref)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = conn_ref->user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  return ctx->qconn;\n}\n\n#ifdef DEBUG_NGTCP2\nstatic void quic_printf(void *user_data, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n\n  (void)ctx;  /* need an easy handle to infof() message */\n  va_list ap;\n  va_start(ap, fmt);\n  curl_mvfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n  va_end(ap);\n  curl_mfprintf(stderr, \"\\n\");\n}\n#endif\n\nstatic void qlog_callback(void *user_data, uint32_t flags,\n                          const void *data, size_t datalen)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  (void)flags;\n  if(ctx->qlogfd != -1) {\n    ssize_t rc = write(ctx->qlogfd, data, datalen);\n    if(rc == -1) {\n      /* on write error, stop further write attempts */\n      curlx_close(ctx->qlogfd);\n      ctx->qlogfd = -1;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nstatic void quic_settings(struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx,\n                          struct Curl_easy *data,\n                          struct pkt_io_ctx *pktx)\n{\n  ngtcp2_settings *s = &ctx->settings;\n  ngtcp2_transport_params *t = &ctx->transport_params;\n\n  ngtcp2_settings_default(s);\n  ngtcp2_transport_params_default(t);\n#ifdef DEBUG_NGTCP2\n  s->log_printf = quic_printf;\n#else\n  s->log_printf = NULL;\n#endif\n\n  s->initial_ts = pktx->ts;\n  s->handshake_timeout = (data->set.connecttimeout > 0) ?\n    data->set.connecttimeout * NGTCP2_MILLISECONDS : QUIC_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT;\n  s->max_window = H3_CONN_WINDOW_SIZE_MAX;\n  s->max_stream_window = 0; /* disable ngtcp2 auto-tuning of window */\n  s->no_pmtud = FALSE;\n#ifdef NGTCP2_SETTINGS_V3\n  /* try ten times the ngtcp2 defaults here for problems with Caddy */\n  s->glitch_ratelim_burst = 1000 * 10;\n  s->glitch_ratelim_rate = 33 * 10;\n#endif\n  t->initial_max_data = s->max_window;\n  t->initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local = H3_STREAM_WINDOW_SIZE_INITIAL;\n  t->initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote = H3_STREAM_WINDOW_SIZE_INITIAL;\n  t->initial_max_stream_data_uni = t->initial_max_data;\n  t->initial_max_streams_bidi = QUIC_MAX_STREAMS;\n  t->initial_max_streams_uni = QUIC_MAX_STREAMS;\n  t->max_idle_timeout = 0; /* no idle timeout from our side */\n  if(ctx->qlogfd != -1) {\n    s->qlog_write = qlog_callback;\n  }\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode init_ngh3_conn(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                               struct Curl_easy *data);\n\nstatic int cf_ngtcp2_handshake_completed(ngtcp2_conn *tconn, void *user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf ? cf->ctx : NULL;\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n\n  (void)tconn;\n  DEBUGASSERT(ctx);\n  data = CF_DATA_CURRENT(cf);\n  DEBUGASSERT(data);\n  if(!ctx || !data)\n    return NGHTTP3_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n\n  ctx->handshake_at = *Curl_pgrs_now(data);\n  ctx->tls_handshake_complete = TRUE;\n  Curl_vquic_report_handshake(&ctx->tls, cf, data);\n\n  ctx->tls_vrfy_result = Curl_vquic_tls_verify_peer(&ctx->tls, cf,\n                                                    data, &ctx->peer);\n#ifdef CURLVERBOSE\n  if(Curl_trc_is_verbose(data)) {\n    const ngtcp2_transport_params *rp;\n    rp = ngtcp2_conn_get_remote_transport_params(ctx->qconn);\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"handshake complete after %\" FMT_TIMEDIFF_T\n                \"ms, remote transport[max_udp_payload=%\" PRIu64\n                \", initial_max_data=%\" PRIu64\n                \"]\",\n               curlx_ptimediff_ms(&ctx->handshake_at, &ctx->started_at),\n               rp->max_udp_payload_size, rp->initial_max_data);\n  }\n#endif\n\n  /* In case of earlydata, where we simulate being connected, update\n   * the handshake time when we really did connect */\n  if(ctx->use_earlydata)\n    Curl_pgrsTimeWas(data, TIMER_APPCONNECT, ctx->handshake_at);\n  if(ctx->use_earlydata) {\n#if defined(USE_OPENSSL) && defined(HAVE_OPENSSL_EARLYDATA)\n    ctx->earlydata_accepted =\n      (SSL_get_early_data_status(ctx->tls.ossl.ssl) !=\n       SSL_EARLY_DATA_REJECTED);\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_GNUTLS\n    int flags = gnutls_session_get_flags(ctx->tls.gtls.session);\n    ctx->earlydata_accepted = !!(flags & GNUTLS_SFLAGS_EARLY_DATA);\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_WOLFSSL\n#ifdef WOLFSSL_EARLY_DATA\n    ctx->earlydata_accepted =\n      (wolfSSL_get_early_data_status(ctx->tls.wssl.ssl) !=\n       WOLFSSL_EARLY_DATA_REJECTED);\n#else\n    DEBUGASSERT(0); /* should not come here if ED is disabled. */\n    ctx->earlydata_accepted = FALSE;\n#endif /* WOLFSSL_EARLY_DATA */\n#endif\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"server did%s accept %zu bytes of early data\",\n                ctx->earlydata_accepted ? \"\" : \" not\", ctx->earlydata_skip);\n    Curl_pgrsEarlyData(data, ctx->earlydata_accepted ?\n                              (curl_off_t)ctx->earlydata_skip :\n                             -(curl_off_t)ctx->earlydata_skip);\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_conn_close(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                 struct Curl_easy *data);\n\nstatic bool cf_ngtcp2_err_is_fatal(int code)\n{\n  return (NGTCP2_ERR_FATAL >= code) ||\n         (NGTCP2_ERR_DROP_CONN == code) ||\n         (NGTCP2_ERR_IDLE_CLOSE == code);\n}\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_err_set(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                              struct Curl_easy *data, int code)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  if(!ctx->last_error.error_code) {\n    if(NGTCP2_ERR_CRYPTO == code) {\n      ngtcp2_ccerr_set_tls_alert(&ctx->last_error,\n                                 ngtcp2_conn_get_tls_alert(ctx->qconn),\n                                 NULL, 0);\n    }\n    else {\n      ngtcp2_ccerr_set_liberr(&ctx->last_error, code, NULL, 0);\n    }\n  }\n  if(cf_ngtcp2_err_is_fatal(code))\n    cf_ngtcp2_conn_close(cf, data);\n}\n\nstatic bool cf_ngtcp2_h3_err_is_fatal(int code)\n{\n  return (NGHTTP3_ERR_FATAL >= code) ||\n         (NGHTTP3_ERR_H3_CLOSED_CRITICAL_STREAM == code);\n}\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_h3_err_set(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                 struct Curl_easy *data, int code)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  if(!ctx->last_error.error_code) {\n    ngtcp2_ccerr_set_application_error(&ctx->last_error,\n      nghttp3_err_infer_quic_app_error_code(code), NULL, 0);\n  }\n  if(cf_ngtcp2_h3_err_is_fatal(code))\n    cf_ngtcp2_conn_close(cf, data);\n}\n\nstatic int cb_recv_stream_data(ngtcp2_conn *tconn, uint32_t flags,\n                               int64_t stream_id, uint64_t offset,\n                               const uint8_t *buf, size_t buflen,\n                               void *user_data, void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  nghttp3_ssize rc;\n  uint64_t nconsumed;\n  int fin = (flags & NGTCP2_STREAM_DATA_FLAG_FIN) ? 1 : 0;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = stream_user_data;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n  (void)offset;\n\n  rc = nghttp3_conn_read_stream(ctx->h3conn, stream_id, buf, buflen, fin);\n  if(rc < 0) {\n    if(data && stream) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] error on known stream, \"\n                  \"reset=%d, closed=%d\",\n                  stream_id, stream->reset, stream->closed);\n    }\n    return NGTCP2_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n  }\n  nconsumed = (uint64_t)rc;\n  if(nconsumed) {\n    /* number of bytes inside buflen which consists of framing overhead\n     * including QPACK HEADERS. In other words, it does not consume payload of\n     * DATA frame. */\n    ngtcp2_conn_extend_max_stream_offset(tconn, stream_id, nconsumed);\n    ngtcp2_conn_extend_max_offset(tconn, nconsumed);\n    if(stream) {\n      stream->rx_offset += nconsumed;\n      stream->rx_offset_max += nconsumed;\n    }\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_acked_stream_data_offset(ngtcp2_conn *tconn, int64_t stream_id,\n                                       uint64_t offset, uint64_t datalen,\n                                       void *user_data, void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  int rv;\n  (void)stream_id;\n  (void)tconn;\n  (void)offset;\n  (void)datalen;\n  (void)stream_user_data;\n\n  rv = nghttp3_conn_add_ack_offset(ctx->h3conn, stream_id, datalen);\n  if(rv && rv != NGHTTP3_ERR_STREAM_NOT_FOUND) {\n    return NGTCP2_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n  }\n\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_stream_close(ngtcp2_conn *tconn, uint32_t flags,\n                           int64_t stream_id, uint64_t app_error_code,\n                           void *user_data, void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = stream_user_data;\n  int rv;\n\n  (void)tconn;\n  /* stream is closed... */\n  if(!data)\n    data = CF_DATA_CURRENT(cf);\n  if(!data)\n    return NGTCP2_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n\n  if(!(flags & NGTCP2_STREAM_CLOSE_FLAG_APP_ERROR_CODE_SET)) {\n    app_error_code = NGHTTP3_H3_NO_ERROR;\n  }\n\n  rv = nghttp3_conn_close_stream(ctx->h3conn, stream_id, app_error_code);\n  CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] quic close(app_error=%\"\n              PRIu64 \") -> %d\", stream_id, app_error_code, rv);\n  if(rv && rv != NGHTTP3_ERR_STREAM_NOT_FOUND) {\n    cf_ngtcp2_h3_err_set(cf, data, rv);\n    return NGTCP2_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n  }\n\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_stream_reset(ngtcp2_conn *tconn, int64_t stream_id,\n                           uint64_t final_size, uint64_t app_error_code,\n                           void *user_data, void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = stream_user_data;\n  int rv;\n  (void)tconn;\n  (void)final_size;\n  (void)app_error_code;\n\n  rv = nghttp3_conn_shutdown_stream_read(ctx->h3conn, stream_id);\n  CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] reset -> %d\", stream_id, rv);\n  if(rv && rv != NGHTTP3_ERR_STREAM_NOT_FOUND) {\n    return NGTCP2_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n  }\n\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_stream_stop_sending(ngtcp2_conn *tconn, int64_t stream_id,\n                                  uint64_t app_error_code, void *user_data,\n                                  void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  int rv;\n  (void)tconn;\n  (void)app_error_code;\n  (void)stream_user_data;\n\n  rv = nghttp3_conn_shutdown_stream_read(ctx->h3conn, stream_id);\n  if(rv && rv != NGHTTP3_ERR_STREAM_NOT_FOUND) {\n    return NGTCP2_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n  }\n\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_extend_max_local_streams_bidi(ngtcp2_conn *tconn,\n                                            uint64_t max_streams,\n                                            void *user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = CF_DATA_CURRENT(cf);\n\n  (void)tconn;\n  ctx->max_bidi_streams = max_streams;\n  if(data)\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"max bidi streams now %\" PRIu64 \", used %\" PRIu64,\n                ctx->max_bidi_streams, ctx->used_bidi_streams);\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_extend_max_stream_data(ngtcp2_conn *tconn, int64_t stream_id,\n                                     uint64_t max_data, void *user_data,\n                                     void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct Curl_easy *s_data = stream_user_data;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream;\n  int rv;\n  (void)tconn;\n  (void)max_data;\n\n  rv = nghttp3_conn_unblock_stream(ctx->h3conn, stream_id);\n  if(rv && rv != NGHTTP3_ERR_STREAM_NOT_FOUND) {\n    return NGTCP2_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n  }\n  stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, s_data);\n  if(stream && stream->quic_flow_blocked) {\n    CURL_TRC_CF(s_data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] unblock quic flow\", stream_id);\n    stream->quic_flow_blocked = FALSE;\n    Curl_multi_mark_dirty(s_data);\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic void cb_rand(uint8_t *dest, size_t destlen,\n                    const ngtcp2_rand_ctx *rand_ctx)\n{\n  CURLcode result;\n  (void)rand_ctx;\n\n  result = Curl_rand(NULL, dest, destlen);\n  if(result) {\n    /* cb_rand is only used for non-cryptographic context. If Curl_rand\n       failed, just fill 0 and call it *random*. */\n    memset(dest, 0, destlen);\n  }\n}\n\nstatic int cb_get_new_connection_id(ngtcp2_conn *tconn, ngtcp2_cid *cid,\n                                    uint8_t *token, size_t cidlen,\n                                    void *user_data)\n{\n  CURLcode result;\n  (void)tconn;\n  (void)user_data;\n\n  result = Curl_rand(NULL, cid->data, cidlen);\n  if(result)\n    return NGTCP2_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n  cid->datalen = cidlen;\n\n  result = Curl_rand(NULL, token, NGTCP2_STATELESS_RESET_TOKENLEN);\n  if(result)\n    return NGTCP2_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_recv_rx_key(ngtcp2_conn *tconn, ngtcp2_encryption_level level,\n                          void *user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf ? cf->ctx : NULL;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = CF_DATA_CURRENT(cf);\n  (void)tconn;\n\n  if(level != NGTCP2_ENCRYPTION_LEVEL_1RTT)\n    return 0;\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(ctx);\n  DEBUGASSERT(data);\n  if(ctx && data && !ctx->h3conn) {\n    if(init_ngh3_conn(cf, data))\n      return NGTCP2_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\n#if defined(_MSC_VER) && defined(_DLL)\n#pragma warning(push)\n#pragma warning(disable:4232) /* MSVC extension, dllimport identity */\n#endif\n\nstatic ngtcp2_callbacks ng_callbacks = {\n  ngtcp2_crypto_client_initial_cb,\n  NULL, /* recv_client_initial */\n  ngtcp2_crypto_recv_crypto_data_cb,\n  cf_ngtcp2_handshake_completed,\n  NULL, /* recv_version_negotiation */\n  ngtcp2_crypto_encrypt_cb,\n  ngtcp2_crypto_decrypt_cb,\n  ngtcp2_crypto_hp_mask_cb,\n  cb_recv_stream_data,\n  cb_acked_stream_data_offset,\n  NULL, /* stream_open */\n  cb_stream_close,\n  NULL, /* recv_stateless_reset */\n  ngtcp2_crypto_recv_retry_cb,\n  cb_extend_max_local_streams_bidi,\n  NULL, /* extend_max_local_streams_uni */\n  cb_rand,\n  cb_get_new_connection_id,\n  NULL, /* remove_connection_id */\n  ngtcp2_crypto_update_key_cb, /* update_key */\n  NULL, /* path_validation */\n  NULL, /* select_preferred_addr */\n  cb_stream_reset,\n  NULL, /* extend_max_remote_streams_bidi */\n  NULL, /* extend_max_remote_streams_uni */\n  cb_extend_max_stream_data,\n  NULL, /* dcid_status */\n  NULL, /* handshake_confirmed */\n  NULL, /* recv_new_token */\n  ngtcp2_crypto_delete_crypto_aead_ctx_cb,\n  ngtcp2_crypto_delete_crypto_cipher_ctx_cb,\n  NULL, /* recv_datagram */\n  NULL, /* ack_datagram */\n  NULL, /* lost_datagram */\n  ngtcp2_crypto_get_path_challenge_data_cb,\n  cb_stream_stop_sending,\n  NULL, /* version_negotiation */\n  cb_recv_rx_key,\n  NULL, /* recv_tx_key */\n  NULL, /* early_data_rejected */\n#ifdef NGTCP2_CALLBACKS_V2  /* ngtcp2 v1.14.0+ */\n  NULL, /* begin_path_validation */\n#endif\n};\n\n#if defined(_MSC_VER) && defined(_DLL)\n#pragma warning(pop)\n#endif\n\n/**\n * Connection maintenance like timeouts on packet ACKs etc. are done by us, not\n * the OS like for TCP. POLL events on the socket therefore are not\n * sufficient.\n * ngtcp2 tells us when it wants to be invoked again. We handle that via\n * the `Curl_expire()` mechanisms.\n */\nstatic CURLcode check_and_set_expiry(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                     struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                     struct pkt_io_ctx *pktx)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct pkt_io_ctx local_pktx;\n  ngtcp2_tstamp expiry;\n\n  if(!pktx) {\n    pktx_init(&local_pktx, cf, data);\n    pktx = &local_pktx;\n  }\n  else {\n    pktx_update_time(data, pktx, cf);\n  }\n\n  expiry = ngtcp2_conn_get_expiry(ctx->qconn);\n  if(expiry != UINT64_MAX) {\n    if(expiry <= pktx->ts) {\n      CURLcode result;\n      int rv = ngtcp2_conn_handle_expiry(ctx->qconn, pktx->ts);\n      if(rv) {\n        failf(data, \"ngtcp2_conn_handle_expiry returned error: %s\",\n              ngtcp2_strerror(rv));\n        cf_ngtcp2_err_set(cf, data, rv);\n        return CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n      }\n      result = cf_progress_ingress(cf, data, pktx);\n      if(result)\n        return result;\n      result = cf_progress_egress(cf, data, pktx);\n      if(result)\n        return result;\n      /* ask again, things might have changed */\n      expiry = ngtcp2_conn_get_expiry(ctx->qconn);\n    }\n\n    if(expiry > pktx->ts) {\n      ngtcp2_duration timeout = expiry - pktx->ts;\n      if(timeout % NGTCP2_MILLISECONDS) {\n        timeout += NGTCP2_MILLISECONDS;\n      }\n      Curl_expire(data, (timediff_t)(timeout / NGTCP2_MILLISECONDS),\n                  EXPIRE_QUIC);\n    }\n  }\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode cf_ngtcp2_adjust_pollset(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                         struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                         struct easy_pollset *ps)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  bool want_recv, want_send;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n\n  if(!ctx->qconn)\n    return CURLE_OK;\n\n  Curl_pollset_check(data, ps, ctx->q.sockfd, &want_recv, &want_send);\n  if(!want_send && !Curl_bufq_is_empty(&ctx->q.sendbuf))\n    want_send = TRUE;\n\n  if(want_recv || want_send) {\n    struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n    struct cf_call_data save;\n    bool c_exhaust, s_exhaust;\n\n    CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n    c_exhaust = want_send && (!ngtcp2_conn_get_cwnd_left(ctx->qconn) ||\n                              !ngtcp2_conn_get_max_data_left(ctx->qconn));\n    s_exhaust = want_send && stream && stream->id >= 0 &&\n                stream->quic_flow_blocked;\n    want_recv = (want_recv || c_exhaust || s_exhaust);\n    want_send = (!s_exhaust && want_send) ||\n                 !Curl_bufq_is_empty(&ctx->q.sendbuf);\n\n    result = Curl_pollset_set(data, ps, ctx->q.sockfd, want_recv, want_send);\n    CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save);\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_h3_stream_close(nghttp3_conn *conn, int64_t stream_id,\n                              uint64_t app_error_code, void *user_data,\n                              void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = stream_user_data;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n  (void)conn;\n  (void)stream_id;\n\n  /* we might be called by nghttp3 after we already cleaned up */\n  if(!stream)\n    return 0;\n\n  stream->closed = TRUE;\n  stream->error3 = app_error_code;\n  if(stream->error3 != NGHTTP3_H3_NO_ERROR) {\n    stream->reset = TRUE;\n    stream->send_closed = TRUE;\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] RESET: error %\" PRIu64,\n                stream->id, stream->error3);\n  }\n  else {\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] CLOSED\", stream->id);\n  }\n  Curl_multi_mark_dirty(data);\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic void h3_xfer_write_resp_hd(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                  struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream,\n                                  const char *buf, size_t blen, bool eos)\n{\n  /* This function returns no error intentionally, but records\n   * the result at the stream, skipping further writes once the\n   * `result` of the transfer is known.\n   * The stream is subsequently cancelled \"higher up\" in the filter's\n   * send/recv callbacks. Closing the stream here leads to SEND/RECV\n   * errors in other places that then overwrite the transfer's result. */\n  if(!stream->xfer_result) {\n    stream->xfer_result = Curl_xfer_write_resp_hd(data, buf, blen, eos);\n    if(stream->xfer_result)\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] error %d writing %zu \"\n                  \"bytes of headers\", stream->id, stream->xfer_result, blen);\n  }\n}\n\nstatic void h3_xfer_write_resp(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                               struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               struct h3_stream_ctx *stream,\n                               const char *buf, size_t blen, bool eos)\n{\n  /* This function returns no error intentionally, but records\n   * the result at the stream, skipping further writes once the\n   * `result` of the transfer is known.\n   * The stream is subsequently cancelled \"higher up\" in the filter's\n   * send/recv callbacks. Closing the stream here leads to SEND/RECV\n   * errors in other places that then overwrite the transfer's result. */\n  if(!stream->xfer_result) {\n    stream->xfer_result = Curl_xfer_write_resp(data, buf, blen, eos);\n    /* If the transfer write is errored, we do not want any more data */\n    if(stream->xfer_result) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] error %d writing %zu bytes of data\",\n                  stream->id, stream->xfer_result, blen);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_upd_rx_win(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                 struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                 struct h3_stream_ctx *stream)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  uint64_t cur_win, wanted_win = H3_STREAM_WINDOW_SIZE_MAX;\n\n  /* how much does rate limiting allow us to acknowledge? */\n  if(Curl_rlimit_active(&data->progress.dl.rlimit)) {\n    int64_t avail;\n\n    /* start rate limit updates only after first bytes arrived */\n    if(!stream->rx_offset)\n      return;\n\n    avail = Curl_rlimit_avail(&data->progress.dl.rlimit,\n                              Curl_pgrs_now(data));\n    if(avail <= 0) {\n      /* nothing available, do not extend the rx offset */\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] dl rate limit exhausted (%\" PRId64\n                  \" tokens)\", stream->id, avail);\n      return;\n    }\n    wanted_win = CURLMIN((uint64_t)avail, H3_STREAM_WINDOW_SIZE_MAX);\n  }\n\n  if(stream->rx_offset_max < stream->rx_offset) {\n    DEBUGASSERT(0);\n    return;\n  }\n  cur_win = stream->rx_offset_max - stream->rx_offset;\n\n  if(wanted_win > cur_win) {\n    uint64_t delta = wanted_win - cur_win;\n\n    if(UINT64_MAX - delta < stream->rx_offset_max)\n      delta = UINT64_MAX - stream->rx_offset_max;\n    if(delta) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] rx window, extend by %\" PRIu64\n                  \" bytes\", stream->id, delta);\n      stream->rx_offset_max += delta;\n      ngtcp2_conn_extend_max_stream_offset(ctx->qconn, stream->id, delta);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nstatic int cb_h3_recv_data(nghttp3_conn *conn, int64_t stream3_id,\n                           const uint8_t *buf, size_t blen,\n                           void *user_data, void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = stream_user_data;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n\n  (void)conn;\n  (void)stream3_id;\n\n  if(!stream)\n    return NGHTTP3_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n\n  h3_xfer_write_resp(cf, data, stream, (const char *)buf, blen, FALSE);\n\n  ngtcp2_conn_extend_max_offset(ctx->qconn, blen);\n  stream->rx_offset += blen;\n  if(stream->rx_offset_max < stream->rx_offset)\n    stream->rx_offset_max = stream->rx_offset;\n\n  CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] DATA len=%zu, rx win=%\" PRId64,\n              stream->id, blen, stream->rx_offset_max - stream->rx_offset);\n  cf_ngtcp2_upd_rx_win(cf, data, stream);\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_h3_deferred_consume(nghttp3_conn *conn, int64_t stream3_id,\n                                  size_t consumed, void *user_data,\n                                  void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = stream_user_data;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n  (void)conn;\n\n  /* nghttp3 has consumed bytes on the QUIC stream and we need to\n   * tell the QUIC connection to increase its flow control */\n  ngtcp2_conn_extend_max_stream_offset(ctx->qconn, stream3_id, consumed);\n  ngtcp2_conn_extend_max_offset(ctx->qconn, consumed);\n  if(stream) {\n    stream->rx_offset += consumed;\n    stream->rx_offset_max += consumed;\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_h3_end_headers(nghttp3_conn *conn, int64_t stream_id,\n                             int fin, void *user_data, void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = stream_user_data;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n  (void)conn;\n  (void)stream_id;\n  (void)fin;\n  (void)cf;\n\n  if(!stream)\n    return 0;\n  /* add a CRLF only if we have received some headers */\n  h3_xfer_write_resp_hd(cf, data, stream, STRCONST(\"\\r\\n\"),\n                        (bool)stream->closed);\n\n  CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] end_headers, status=%d\",\n              stream_id, stream->status_code);\n  if(stream->status_code / 100 != 1) {\n    stream->resp_hds_complete = TRUE;\n  }\n  Curl_multi_mark_dirty(data);\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_h3_recv_header(nghttp3_conn *conn, int64_t stream_id,\n                             int32_t token, nghttp3_rcbuf *name,\n                             nghttp3_rcbuf *value, uint8_t flags,\n                             void *user_data, void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  nghttp3_vec h3name = nghttp3_rcbuf_get_buf(name);\n  nghttp3_vec h3val = nghttp3_rcbuf_get_buf(value);\n  struct Curl_easy *data = stream_user_data;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  (void)conn;\n  (void)stream_id;\n  (void)token;\n  (void)flags;\n  (void)cf;\n\n  /* we might have cleaned up this transfer already */\n  if(!stream)\n    return 0;\n\n  if(token == NGHTTP3_QPACK_TOKEN__STATUS) {\n\n    result = Curl_http_decode_status(&stream->status_code,\n                                     (const char *)h3val.base, h3val.len);\n    if(result)\n      return NGHTTP3_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n    curlx_dyn_reset(&ctx->scratch);\n    result = curlx_dyn_addn(&ctx->scratch, STRCONST(\"HTTP/3 \"));\n    if(!result)\n      result = curlx_dyn_addn(&ctx->scratch,\n                              (const char *)h3val.base, h3val.len);\n    if(!result)\n      result = curlx_dyn_addn(&ctx->scratch, STRCONST(\" \\r\\n\"));\n    if(!result)\n      h3_xfer_write_resp_hd(cf, data, stream, curlx_dyn_ptr(&ctx->scratch),\n                            curlx_dyn_len(&ctx->scratch), FALSE);\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] status: %s\",\n                stream_id, curlx_dyn_ptr(&ctx->scratch));\n    if(result) {\n      return NGHTTP3_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n    }\n  }\n  else {\n    /* store as an HTTP1-style header */\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] header: %.*s: %.*s\",\n                stream_id, (int)h3name.len, h3name.base,\n                (int)h3val.len, h3val.base);\n    curlx_dyn_reset(&ctx->scratch);\n    result = curlx_dyn_addn(&ctx->scratch,\n                            (const char *)h3name.base, h3name.len);\n    if(!result)\n      result = curlx_dyn_addn(&ctx->scratch, STRCONST(\": \"));\n    if(!result)\n      result = curlx_dyn_addn(&ctx->scratch,\n                              (const char *)h3val.base, h3val.len);\n    if(!result)\n      result = curlx_dyn_addn(&ctx->scratch, STRCONST(\"\\r\\n\"));\n    if(!result)\n      h3_xfer_write_resp_hd(cf, data, stream, curlx_dyn_ptr(&ctx->scratch),\n                            curlx_dyn_len(&ctx->scratch), FALSE);\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_h3_stop_sending(nghttp3_conn *conn, int64_t stream_id,\n                              uint64_t app_error_code, void *user_data,\n                              void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  int rv;\n  (void)conn;\n  (void)stream_user_data;\n\n  rv = ngtcp2_conn_shutdown_stream_read(ctx->qconn, 0, stream_id,\n                                        app_error_code);\n  if(rv && rv != NGTCP2_ERR_STREAM_NOT_FOUND) {\n    return NGHTTP3_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n  }\n\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_h3_reset_stream(nghttp3_conn *conn, int64_t stream_id,\n                              uint64_t app_error_code, void *user_data,\n                              void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = stream_user_data;\n  int rv;\n  (void)conn;\n\n  rv = ngtcp2_conn_shutdown_stream_write(ctx->qconn, 0, stream_id,\n                                         app_error_code);\n  CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] reset -> %d\", stream_id, rv);\n  if(rv && rv != NGTCP2_ERR_STREAM_NOT_FOUND) {\n    return NGHTTP3_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n  }\n\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic nghttp3_callbacks ngh3_callbacks = {\n  cb_h3_acked_req_body, /* acked_stream_data */\n  cb_h3_stream_close,\n  cb_h3_recv_data,\n  cb_h3_deferred_consume,\n  NULL, /* begin_headers */\n  cb_h3_recv_header,\n  cb_h3_end_headers,\n  NULL, /* begin_trailers */\n  cb_h3_recv_header,\n  NULL, /* end_trailers */\n  cb_h3_stop_sending,\n  NULL, /* end_stream */\n  cb_h3_reset_stream,\n  NULL, /* shutdown */\n  NULL, /* recv_settings (deprecated) */\n#ifdef NGHTTP3_CALLBACKS_V2  /* nghttp3 v1.11.0+ */\n  NULL, /* recv_origin */\n  NULL, /* end_origin */\n  NULL, /* rand */\n#endif\n#ifdef NGHTTP3_CALLBACKS_V3  /* nghttp3 v1.14.0+ */\n  NULL, /* recv_settings2 */\n#endif\n};\n\nstatic CURLcode init_ngh3_conn(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                               struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  int64_t ctrl_stream_id, qpack_enc_stream_id, qpack_dec_stream_id;\n  int rc;\n\n  if(ngtcp2_conn_get_streams_uni_left(ctx->qconn) < 3) {\n    failf(data, \"QUIC connection lacks 3 uni streams to run HTTP/3\");\n    return CURLE_QUIC_CONNECT_ERROR;\n  }\n\n  nghttp3_settings_default(&ctx->h3settings);\n\n  rc = nghttp3_conn_client_new(&ctx->h3conn,\n                               &ngh3_callbacks,\n                               &ctx->h3settings,\n                               Curl_nghttp3_mem(),\n                               cf);\n  if(rc) {\n    failf(data, \"error creating nghttp3 connection instance\");\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n  }\n\n  rc = ngtcp2_conn_open_uni_stream(ctx->qconn, &ctrl_stream_id, NULL);\n  if(rc) {\n    failf(data, \"error creating HTTP/3 control stream: %s\",\n          ngtcp2_strerror(rc));\n    return CURLE_QUIC_CONNECT_ERROR;\n  }\n\n  rc = nghttp3_conn_bind_control_stream(ctx->h3conn, ctrl_stream_id);\n  if(rc) {\n    failf(data, \"error binding HTTP/3 control stream: %s\",\n          ngtcp2_strerror(rc));\n    return CURLE_QUIC_CONNECT_ERROR;\n  }\n\n  rc = ngtcp2_conn_open_uni_stream(ctx->qconn, &qpack_enc_stream_id, NULL);\n  if(rc) {\n    failf(data, \"error creating HTTP/3 qpack encoding stream: %s\",\n          ngtcp2_strerror(rc));\n    return CURLE_QUIC_CONNECT_ERROR;\n  }\n\n  rc = ngtcp2_conn_open_uni_stream(ctx->qconn, &qpack_dec_stream_id, NULL);\n  if(rc) {\n    failf(data, \"error creating HTTP/3 qpack decoding stream: %s\",\n          ngtcp2_strerror(rc));\n    return CURLE_QUIC_CONNECT_ERROR;\n  }\n\n  rc = nghttp3_conn_bind_qpack_streams(ctx->h3conn, qpack_enc_stream_id,\n                                       qpack_dec_stream_id);\n  if(rc) {\n    failf(data, \"error binding HTTP/3 qpack streams: %s\",\n          ngtcp2_strerror(rc));\n    return CURLE_QUIC_CONNECT_ERROR;\n  }\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode recv_closed_stream(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                   struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                   struct h3_stream_ctx *stream,\n                                   size_t *pnread)\n{\n  (void)cf;\n  *pnread = 0;\n  if(stream->reset) {\n    if(stream->error3 == CURL_H3_ERR_REQUEST_REJECTED) {\n      infof(data, \"HTTP/3 stream %\" PRId64 \" refused by server, try again \"\n            \"on a new connection\", stream->id);\n      connclose(cf->conn, \"REFUSED_STREAM\"); /* do not use this anymore */\n      data->state.refused_stream = TRUE;\n      return CURLE_RECV_ERROR; /* trigger Curl_retry_request() later */\n    }\n    else if(stream->resp_hds_complete && data->req.no_body) {\n        CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] error after response headers, \"\n                    \"but we did not want a body anyway, ignore error 0x%\"\n                    PRIx64 \" %s\", stream->id, stream->error3,\n                    vquic_h3_err_str(stream->error3));\n        return CURLE_OK;\n    }\n    failf(data, \"HTTP/3 stream %\" PRId64 \" reset by server (error 0x%\" PRIx64\n          \" %s)\", stream->id, stream->error3,\n          vquic_h3_err_str(stream->error3));\n    return data->req.bytecount ? CURLE_PARTIAL_FILE : CURLE_HTTP3;\n  }\n  else if(!stream->resp_hds_complete) {\n    failf(data,\n          \"HTTP/3 stream %\" PRId64 \" was closed cleanly, but before \"\n          \"getting all response header fields, treated as error\",\n          stream->id);\n    return CURLE_HTTP3;\n  }\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\n/* incoming data frames on the h3 stream */\nstatic CURLcode cf_ngtcp2_recv(struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               char *buf, size_t blen, size_t *pnread)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n  struct cf_call_data save;\n  struct pkt_io_ctx pktx;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  int i;\n\n  (void)ctx;\n  (void)buf;\n  NOVERBOSE((void)blen);\n\n  CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n  DEBUGASSERT(cf->connected);\n  DEBUGASSERT(ctx);\n  DEBUGASSERT(ctx->qconn);\n  DEBUGASSERT(ctx->h3conn);\n  *pnread = 0;\n\n  /* handshake verification failed in callback, do not recv anything */\n  if(ctx->tls_vrfy_result) {\n    result = ctx->tls_vrfy_result;\n    goto denied;\n  }\n\n  pktx_init(&pktx, cf, data);\n\n  if(!stream || ctx->shutdown_started) {\n    result = CURLE_RECV_ERROR;\n    goto out;\n  }\n\n  cf_ngtcp2_upd_rx_win(cf, data, stream);\n\n  /* first check for results/closed already known without touching\n   * the connection. For an already failed/closed stream, errors on\n   * the connection do not count.\n   * Then handle incoming data and check for failed/closed again.\n   */\n  for(i = 0; i < 2; ++i) {\n    if(stream->xfer_result) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] xfer write failed\", stream->id);\n      cf_ngtcp2_stream_close(cf, data, stream);\n      result = stream->xfer_result;\n      goto out;\n    }\n    else if(stream->closed) {\n      result = recv_closed_stream(cf, data, stream, pnread);\n      goto out;\n    }\n\n    if(!i && cf_progress_ingress(cf, data, &pktx)) {\n      result = CURLE_RECV_ERROR;\n      goto out;\n    }\n  }\n\n  result = CURLE_AGAIN;\n\nout:\n  result = Curl_1st_err(result, cf_progress_egress(cf, data, &pktx));\n  result = Curl_1st_err(result, check_and_set_expiry(cf, data, &pktx));\ndenied:\n  CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] cf_recv(blen=%zu) -> %d, %zu\",\n              stream ? stream->id : -1, blen, result, *pnread);\n  CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save);\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic int cb_h3_acked_req_body(nghttp3_conn *conn, int64_t stream_id,\n                                uint64_t datalen, void *user_data,\n                                void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = stream_user_data;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n  size_t skiplen;\n\n  (void)cf;\n  if(!stream)\n    return 0;\n  /* The server acknowledged `datalen` of bytes from our request body.\n   * This is a delta. We have kept this data in `sendbuf` for\n   * re-transmissions and can free it now. */\n  if(datalen >= (uint64_t)stream->sendbuf_len_in_flight)\n    skiplen = stream->sendbuf_len_in_flight;\n  else\n    skiplen = (size_t)datalen;\n  Curl_bufq_skip(&stream->sendbuf, skiplen);\n  stream->sendbuf_len_in_flight -= skiplen;\n\n  /* Resume upload processing if we have more data to send */\n  if(stream->sendbuf_len_in_flight < Curl_bufq_len(&stream->sendbuf)) {\n    int rv = nghttp3_conn_resume_stream(conn, stream_id);\n    if(rv && rv != NGHTTP3_ERR_STREAM_NOT_FOUND) {\n      return NGHTTP3_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n    }\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic nghttp3_ssize cb_h3_read_req_body(nghttp3_conn *conn, int64_t stream_id,\n                                         nghttp3_vec *vec, size_t veccnt,\n                                         uint32_t *pflags, void *user_data,\n                                         void *stream_user_data)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = user_data;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = stream_user_data;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n  ssize_t nwritten = 0;\n  size_t nvecs = 0;\n  (void)cf;\n  (void)conn;\n  (void)stream_id;\n  (void)user_data;\n  (void)veccnt;\n\n  if(!stream)\n    return NGHTTP3_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE;\n  /* nghttp3 keeps references to the sendbuf data until it is ACKed\n   * by the server (see `cb_h3_acked_req_body()` for updates).\n   * `sendbuf_len_in_flight` is the amount of bytes in `sendbuf`\n   * that we have already passed to nghttp3, but which have not been\n   * ACKed yet.\n   * Any amount beyond `sendbuf_len_in_flight` we need still to pass\n   * to nghttp3. Do that now, if we can. */\n  if(stream->sendbuf_len_in_flight < Curl_bufq_len(&stream->sendbuf)) {\n    nvecs = 0;\n    while(nvecs < veccnt &&\n          Curl_bufq_peek_at(&stream->sendbuf,\n                            stream->sendbuf_len_in_flight,\n                            CURL_UNCONST(&vec[nvecs].base),\n                            &vec[nvecs].len)) {\n      stream->sendbuf_len_in_flight += vec[nvecs].len;\n      nwritten += vec[nvecs].len;\n      ++nvecs;\n    }\n    DEBUGASSERT(nvecs > 0); /* we SHOULD have been be able to peek */\n  }\n\n  if(nwritten > 0 && stream->upload_left != -1)\n    stream->upload_left -= nwritten;\n\n  /* When we stopped sending and everything in `sendbuf` is \"in flight\",\n   * we are at the end of the request body. */\n  if(stream->upload_left == 0) {\n    *pflags = NGHTTP3_DATA_FLAG_EOF;\n    stream->send_closed = TRUE;\n  }\n  else if(!nwritten) {\n    /* Not EOF, and nothing to give, we signal WOULDBLOCK. */\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] read req body -> AGAIN\", stream->id);\n    return NGHTTP3_ERR_WOULDBLOCK;\n  }\n\n  CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] read req body -> \"\n              \"%d vecs%s with %zu (buffered=%zu, left=%\" FMT_OFF_T \")\",\n              stream->id, (int)nvecs,\n              *pflags == NGHTTP3_DATA_FLAG_EOF ? \" EOF\" : \"\",\n              nwritten, Curl_bufq_len(&stream->sendbuf),\n              stream->upload_left);\n  return (nghttp3_ssize)nvecs;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode h3_stream_open(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                               struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               const uint8_t *buf, size_t len,\n                               size_t *pnwritten)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = NULL;\n  int64_t sid;\n  struct dynhds h2_headers;\n  size_t nheader;\n  nghttp3_nv *nva = NULL;\n  int rc = 0;\n  unsigned int i;\n  nghttp3_data_reader reader;\n  nghttp3_data_reader *preader = NULL;\n  CURLcode result;\n\n  *pnwritten = 0;\n  Curl_dynhds_init(&h2_headers, 0, DYN_HTTP_REQUEST);\n\n  result = h3_data_setup(cf, data);\n  if(result)\n    goto out;\n  stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n  DEBUGASSERT(stream);\n  if(!stream) {\n    result = CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n    goto out;\n  }\n\n  result = Curl_h1_req_parse_read(&stream->h1, buf, len, NULL,\n                                  !data->state.http_ignorecustom ?\n                                  data->set.str[STRING_CUSTOMREQUEST] : NULL,\n                                  0, pnwritten);\n  if(result)\n    goto out;\n  if(!stream->h1.done) {\n    /* need more data */\n    goto out;\n  }\n  DEBUGASSERT(stream->h1.req);\n\n  result = Curl_http_req_to_h2(&h2_headers, stream->h1.req, data);\n  if(result)\n    goto out;\n\n  /* no longer needed */\n  Curl_h1_req_parse_free(&stream->h1);\n\n  nheader = Curl_dynhds_count(&h2_headers);\n  nva = curlx_malloc(sizeof(nghttp3_nv) * nheader);\n  if(!nva) {\n    result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    goto out;\n  }\n\n  for(i = 0; i < nheader; ++i) {\n    struct dynhds_entry *e = Curl_dynhds_getn(&h2_headers, i);\n    nva[i].name = (unsigned char *)e->name;\n    nva[i].namelen = e->namelen;\n    nva[i].value = (unsigned char *)e->value;\n    nva[i].valuelen = e->valuelen;\n    nva[i].flags = NGHTTP3_NV_FLAG_NONE;\n  }\n\n  rc = ngtcp2_conn_open_bidi_stream(ctx->qconn, &sid, data);\n  if(rc) {\n    failf(data, \"can get bidi streams\");\n    result = CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n    goto out;\n  }\n  stream->id = sid;\n  ++ctx->used_bidi_streams;\n\n  switch(data->state.httpreq) {\n  case HTTPREQ_POST:\n  case HTTPREQ_POST_FORM:\n  case HTTPREQ_POST_MIME:\n  case HTTPREQ_PUT:\n    /* known request body size or -1 */\n    if(data->state.infilesize != -1)\n      stream->upload_left = data->state.infilesize;\n    else\n      /* data sending without specifying the data amount up front */\n      stream->upload_left = -1; /* unknown */\n    break;\n  default:\n    /* there is not request body */\n    stream->upload_left = 0; /* no request body */\n    break;\n  }\n\n  stream->send_closed = (stream->upload_left == 0);\n  if(!stream->send_closed) {\n    reader.read_data = cb_h3_read_req_body;\n    preader = &reader;\n  }\n\n  rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_request(ctx->h3conn, stream->id,\n                                   nva, nheader, preader, data);\n  if(rc) {\n    switch(rc) {\n    case NGHTTP3_ERR_CONN_CLOSING:\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"h3sid[%\" PRId64 \"] failed to send, \"\n                  \"connection is closing\", stream->id);\n      break;\n    default:\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"h3sid[%\" PRId64 \"] failed to send -> \"\n                  \"%d (%s)\", stream->id, rc, nghttp3_strerror(rc));\n      break;\n    }\n    cf_ngtcp2_stream_close(cf, data, stream);\n    result = CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n    goto out;\n  }\n\n  cf_ngtcp2_upd_rx_win(cf, data, stream);\n\n  /* RFC 9218: Set stream priority if configured */\n#ifdef USE_HTTP3\n  if(data->set.h3_priority_urgency || data->set.h3_priority_incremental) {\n    nghttp3_pri_spec pri_spec;\n    pri_spec.urgency = data->set.h3_priority_urgency;\n    pri_spec.inc = data->set.h3_priority_incremental ? 1 : 0;\n    rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_priority_update(ctx->h3conn, stream->id, &pri_spec);\n    if(rc) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"h3sid[%\" PRId64 \"] failed to set priority: %d (%s)\",\n                  stream->id, rc, nghttp3_strerror(rc));\n      /* Log warning but don't fail the request */\n    }\n  }\n#endif\n\n  if(Curl_trc_is_verbose(data)) {\n    infof(data, \"[HTTP/3] [%\" PRId64 \"] OPENED stream for %s\",\n          stream->id, Curl_bufref_ptr(&data->state.url));\n    for(i = 0; i < nheader; ++i) {\n      infof(data, \"[HTTP/3] [%\" PRId64 \"] [%.*s: %.*s]\", stream->id,\n            (int)nva[i].namelen, nva[i].name,\n            (int)nva[i].valuelen, nva[i].value);\n    }\n  }\n\nout:\n  curlx_free(nva);\n  Curl_dynhds_free(&h2_headers);\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode cf_ngtcp2_send(struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               const uint8_t *buf, size_t len, bool eos,\n                               size_t *pnwritten)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n  struct cf_call_data save;\n  struct pkt_io_ctx pktx;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n\n  CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n  DEBUGASSERT(cf->connected);\n  DEBUGASSERT(ctx->qconn);\n  DEBUGASSERT(ctx->h3conn);\n  pktx_init(&pktx, cf, data);\n  *pnwritten = 0;\n\n  /* handshake verification failed in callback, do not send anything */\n  if(ctx->tls_vrfy_result) {\n    result = ctx->tls_vrfy_result;\n    goto denied;\n  }\n\n  (void)eos; /* use for stream EOF and block handling */\n  result = cf_progress_ingress(cf, data, &pktx);\n  if(result)\n    goto out;\n\n  if(!stream || stream->id < 0) {\n    if(ctx->shutdown_started) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"cannot open stream on closed connection\");\n      result = CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n      goto out;\n    }\n    result = h3_stream_open(cf, data, buf, len, pnwritten);\n    if(result) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"failed to open stream -> %d\", result);\n      goto out;\n    }\n    VERBOSE(stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data));\n  }\n  else if(stream->xfer_result) {\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] xfer write failed\", stream->id);\n    cf_ngtcp2_stream_close(cf, data, stream);\n    result = stream->xfer_result;\n    goto out;\n  }\n  else if(stream->closed) {\n    if(stream->resp_hds_complete) {\n      /* Server decided to close the stream after having sent us a final\n       * response. This is valid if it is not interested in the request\n       * body. This happens on 30x or 40x responses.\n       * We silently discard the data sent, since this is not a transport\n       * error situation. */\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] discarding data\"\n                  \"on closed stream with response\", stream->id);\n      result = CURLE_OK;\n      *pnwritten = len;\n      goto out;\n    }\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] send_body(len=%zu) \"\n                \"-> stream closed\", stream->id, len);\n    result = CURLE_HTTP3;\n    goto out;\n  }\n  else if(ctx->shutdown_started) {\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"cannot send on closed connection\");\n    result = CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n    goto out;\n  }\n  else {\n    result = Curl_bufq_write(&stream->sendbuf, buf, len, pnwritten);\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] cf_send, add to \"\n                \"sendbuf(len=%zu) -> %d, %zu\",\n                stream->id, len, result, *pnwritten);\n    if(result)\n      goto out;\n    (void)nghttp3_conn_resume_stream(ctx->h3conn, stream->id);\n  }\n\n  if(*pnwritten > 0 && !ctx->tls_handshake_complete && ctx->use_earlydata)\n    ctx->earlydata_skip += *pnwritten;\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(!result);\n  result = cf_progress_egress(cf, data, &pktx);\n\nout:\n  result = Curl_1st_err(result, check_and_set_expiry(cf, data, &pktx));\ndenied:\n  CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] cf_send(len=%zu) -> %d, %zu\",\n              stream ? stream->id : -1, len, result, *pnwritten);\n  CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save);\n  return result;\n}\n\nstruct cf_ngtcp2_recv_ctx {\n  struct pkt_io_ctx *pktx;\n  size_t pkt_count;\n};\n\nstatic CURLcode cf_ngtcp2_recv_pkts(const unsigned char *buf, size_t buflen,\n                                    size_t gso_size,\n                                    struct sockaddr_storage *remote_addr,\n                                    socklen_t remote_addrlen, int ecn,\n                                    void *userp)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_recv_ctx *rctx = userp;\n  struct pkt_io_ctx *pktx = rctx->pktx;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = pktx->cf->ctx;\n  ngtcp2_pkt_info pi;\n  ngtcp2_path path;\n  size_t offset, pktlen;\n  int rv;\n\n  if(!rctx->pkt_count) {\n    pktx_update_time(pktx->data, pktx, pktx->cf);\n    ngtcp2_path_storage_zero(&pktx->ps);\n  }\n\n  if(ecn)\n    CURL_TRC_CF(pktx->data, pktx->cf, \"vquic_recv(len=%zu, gso=%zu, ecn=%x)\",\n                buflen, gso_size, ecn);\n  ngtcp2_addr_init(&path.local, (struct sockaddr *)&ctx->q.local_addr,\n                   ctx->q.local_addrlen);\n  ngtcp2_addr_init(&path.remote, (struct sockaddr *)remote_addr,\n                   remote_addrlen);\n  pi.ecn = (uint8_t)ecn;\n\n  for(offset = 0; offset < buflen; offset += gso_size) {\n    rctx->pkt_count++;\n    pktlen = ((offset + gso_size) <= buflen) ? gso_size : (buflen - offset);\n    rv = ngtcp2_conn_read_pkt(ctx->qconn, &path, &pi,\n                              buf + offset, pktlen, pktx->ts);\n    if(rv) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(pktx->data, pktx->cf, \"ingress, read_pkt -> %s (%d)\",\n                  ngtcp2_strerror(rv), rv);\n      cf_ngtcp2_err_set(pktx->cf, pktx->data, rv);\n\n      if(rv == NGTCP2_ERR_CRYPTO)\n        /* this is a \"TLS problem\", but a failed certificate verification\n           is a common reason for this */\n        return CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION;\n      return CURLE_RECV_ERROR;\n    }\n  }\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode cf_progress_ingress(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                    struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                    struct pkt_io_ctx *pktx)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct pkt_io_ctx local_pktx;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_recv_ctx rctx;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n\n  if(!pktx) {\n    pktx_init(&local_pktx, cf, data);\n    pktx = &local_pktx;\n  }\n\n  result = Curl_vquic_tls_before_recv(&ctx->tls, cf, data);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n\n  rctx.pktx = pktx;\n  rctx.pkt_count = 0;\n  return vquic_recv_packets(cf, data, &ctx->q, 1000,\n                            cf_ngtcp2_recv_pkts, &rctx);\n}\n\n/**\n * Read a network packet to send from ngtcp2 into `buf`.\n * Return number of bytes written or -1 with *err set.\n */\nstatic CURLcode read_pkt_to_send(void *userp,\n                                 unsigned char *buf, size_t buflen,\n                                 size_t *pnread)\n{\n  struct pkt_io_ctx *x = userp;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = x->cf->ctx;\n  nghttp3_vec vec[16];\n  nghttp3_ssize veccnt;\n  ngtcp2_ssize ndatalen;\n  uint32_t flags;\n  int64_t stream_id;\n  int fin;\n  ssize_t n;\n\n  *pnread = 0;\n  veccnt = 0;\n  stream_id = -1;\n  fin = 0;\n\n  /* ngtcp2 may want to put several frames from different streams into\n   * this packet. `NGTCP2_WRITE_STREAM_FLAG_MORE` tells it to do so.\n   * When `NGTCP2_ERR_WRITE_MORE` is returned, we *need* to make\n   * another iteration.\n   * When ngtcp2 is happy (because it has no other frame that would fit\n   * or it has nothing more to send), it returns the total length\n   * of the assembled packet. This may be 0 if there was nothing to send. */\n  for(;;) {\n\n    if(ctx->h3conn && ngtcp2_conn_get_max_data_left(ctx->qconn)) {\n      veccnt = nghttp3_conn_writev_stream(ctx->h3conn, &stream_id, &fin, vec,\n                                          CURL_ARRAYSIZE(vec));\n      if(veccnt < 0) {\n        failf(x->data, \"nghttp3_conn_writev_stream returned error: %s\",\n              nghttp3_strerror((int)veccnt));\n        cf_ngtcp2_h3_err_set(x->cf, x->data, (int)veccnt);\n        return CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n      }\n    }\n\n    flags = NGTCP2_WRITE_STREAM_FLAG_MORE |\n            (fin ? NGTCP2_WRITE_STREAM_FLAG_FIN : 0);\n    n = ngtcp2_conn_writev_stream(ctx->qconn, &x->ps.path,\n                                  NULL, buf, buflen,\n                                  &ndatalen, flags, stream_id,\n                                  (const ngtcp2_vec *)vec, veccnt, x->ts);\n    if(n == 0) {\n      /* nothing to send */\n      return CURLE_AGAIN;\n    }\n    else if(n < 0) {\n      switch(n) {\n      case NGTCP2_ERR_STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED: {\n        struct h3_stream_ctx *stream;\n        DEBUGASSERT(ndatalen == -1);\n        nghttp3_conn_block_stream(ctx->h3conn, stream_id);\n        CURL_TRC_CF(x->data, x->cf, \"[%\" PRId64 \"] block quic flow\",\n                    stream_id);\n        stream = cf_ngtcp2_get_stream(ctx, stream_id);\n        if(stream) /* it might be not one of our h3 streams? */\n          stream->quic_flow_blocked = TRUE;\n        n = 0;\n        break;\n      }\n      case NGTCP2_ERR_STREAM_SHUT_WR:\n        DEBUGASSERT(ndatalen == -1);\n        nghttp3_conn_shutdown_stream_write(ctx->h3conn, stream_id);\n        n = 0;\n        break;\n      case NGTCP2_ERR_WRITE_MORE:\n        /* ngtcp2 wants to send more. update the flow of the stream whose data\n         * is in the buffer and continue */\n        DEBUGASSERT(ndatalen >= 0);\n        n = 0;\n        break;\n      default:\n        DEBUGASSERT(ndatalen == -1);\n        failf(x->data, \"ngtcp2_conn_writev_stream returned error: %s\",\n              ngtcp2_strerror((int)n));\n        cf_ngtcp2_err_set(x->cf, x->data, (int)n);\n        return CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n      }\n    }\n\n    if(ndatalen >= 0) {\n      /* we add the amount of data bytes to the flow windows */\n      int rv = nghttp3_conn_add_write_offset(ctx->h3conn, stream_id, ndatalen);\n      if(rv) {\n        failf(x->data, \"nghttp3_conn_add_write_offset returned error: %s\",\n              nghttp3_strerror(rv));\n        return CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n      }\n    }\n\n    if(n > 0) {\n      /* packet assembled, leave */\n      *pnread = (size_t)n;\n      return CURLE_OK;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode cf_progress_egress(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                   struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                   struct pkt_io_ctx *pktx)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  size_t nread;\n  size_t max_payload_size, path_max_payload_size;\n  size_t pktcnt = 0;\n  size_t gsolen = 0;  /* this disables gso until we have a clue */\n  size_t send_quantum;\n  CURLcode curlcode;\n  struct pkt_io_ctx local_pktx;\n\n  if(!pktx) {\n    pktx_init(&local_pktx, cf, data);\n    pktx = &local_pktx;\n  }\n  else {\n    pktx_update_time(data, pktx, cf);\n    ngtcp2_path_storage_zero(&pktx->ps);\n  }\n\n  curlcode = vquic_flush(cf, data, &ctx->q);\n  if(curlcode) {\n    if(curlcode == CURLE_AGAIN) {\n      Curl_expire(data, 1, EXPIRE_QUIC);\n      return CURLE_OK;\n    }\n    return curlcode;\n  }\n\n  /* In UDP, there is a maximum theoretical packet payload length and\n   * a minimum payload length that is \"guaranteed\" to work.\n   * To detect if this minimum payload can be increased, ngtcp2 sends\n   * now and then a packet payload larger than the minimum. It that\n   * is ACKed by the peer, both parties know that it works and\n   * the subsequent packets can use a larger one.\n   * This is called PMTUD (Path Maximum Transmission Unit Discovery).\n   * Since a PMTUD might be rejected right on send, we do not want it\n   * be followed by other packets of lesser size. Because those would\n   * also fail then. So, if we detect a PMTUD while buffering, we flush.\n   */\n  max_payload_size = ngtcp2_conn_get_max_tx_udp_payload_size(ctx->qconn);\n  path_max_payload_size =\n    ngtcp2_conn_get_path_max_tx_udp_payload_size(ctx->qconn);\n  send_quantum = ngtcp2_conn_get_send_quantum(ctx->qconn);\n  CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"egress, collect and send packets, quantum=%zu\",\n              send_quantum);\n  for(;;) {\n    /* add the next packet to send, if any, to our buffer */\n    curlcode = Curl_bufq_sipn(&ctx->q.sendbuf, max_payload_size,\n                              read_pkt_to_send, pktx, &nread);\n    if(curlcode == CURLE_AGAIN)\n      break;\n    else if(curlcode)\n      return curlcode;\n    else {\n      size_t buflen = Curl_bufq_len(&ctx->q.sendbuf);\n      if((buflen >= send_quantum) ||\n         ((buflen + gsolen) >= ctx->q.sendbuf.chunk_size))\n        break;\n      DEBUGASSERT(nread > 0);\n      ++pktcnt;\n      if(pktcnt == 1) {\n        /* first packet in buffer. This is either of a known, \"good\"\n         * payload size or it is a PMTUD. We will see. */\n        gsolen = nread;\n      }\n      else if(nread > gsolen ||\n              (gsolen > path_max_payload_size && nread != gsolen)) {\n        /* The just added packet is a PMTUD *or* the one(s) before the\n         * just added were PMTUD and the last one is smaller.\n         * Flush the buffer before the last add. */\n        curlcode = vquic_send_tail_split(cf, data, &ctx->q,\n                                         gsolen, nread, nread);\n        if(curlcode) {\n          if(curlcode == CURLE_AGAIN) {\n            Curl_expire(data, 1, EXPIRE_QUIC);\n            return CURLE_OK;\n          }\n          return curlcode;\n        }\n        pktcnt = 0;\n      }\n      else if(nread < gsolen) {\n        /* Reached MAX_PKT_BURST *or*\n         * the capacity of our buffer *or*\n         * last add was shorter than the previous ones, flush */\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  if(!Curl_bufq_is_empty(&ctx->q.sendbuf)) {\n    /* time to send */\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"egress, send collected %zu packets in %zu bytes\",\n                pktcnt, Curl_bufq_len(&ctx->q.sendbuf));\n    curlcode = vquic_send(cf, data, &ctx->q, gsolen);\n    if(curlcode) {\n      if(curlcode == CURLE_AGAIN) {\n        Curl_expire(data, 1, EXPIRE_QUIC);\n        return CURLE_OK;\n      }\n      return curlcode;\n    }\n    pktx_update_time(data, pktx, cf);\n    ngtcp2_conn_update_pkt_tx_time(ctx->qconn, pktx->ts);\n  }\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode h3_data_pause(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                              struct Curl_easy *data,\n                              bool pause)\n{\n  /* There seems to exist no API in ngtcp2 to shrink/enlarge the streams\n   * windows. As we do in HTTP/2. */\n  (void)cf;\n  if(!pause)\n    Curl_multi_mark_dirty(data);\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode cf_ngtcp2_cntrl(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                int event, int arg1, void *arg2)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  struct cf_call_data save;\n\n  CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n  (void)arg1;\n  (void)arg2;\n  switch(event) {\n  case CF_CTRL_DATA_SETUP:\n    break;\n  case CF_CTRL_DATA_PAUSE:\n    result = h3_data_pause(cf, data, (arg1 != 0));\n    break;\n  case CF_CTRL_DATA_DONE:\n    h3_data_done(cf, data);\n    break;\n  case CF_CTRL_DATA_DONE_SEND: {\n    struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(ctx, data);\n    if(stream && !stream->send_closed) {\n      stream->send_closed = TRUE;\n      stream->upload_left = Curl_bufq_len(&stream->sendbuf) -\n        stream->sendbuf_len_in_flight;\n      (void)nghttp3_conn_resume_stream(ctx->h3conn, stream->id);\n    }\n    break;\n  }\n  case CF_CTRL_CONN_INFO_UPDATE:\n    if(!cf->sockindex && cf->connected) {\n      cf->conn->httpversion_seen = 30;\n      Curl_conn_set_multiplex(cf->conn);\n    }\n    break;\n  default:\n    break;\n  }\n  CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save);\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_ctx_close(struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx)\n{\n  struct cf_call_data save = ctx->call_data;\n\n  if(!ctx->initialized)\n    return;\n  if(ctx->qlogfd != -1) {\n    curlx_close(ctx->qlogfd);\n  }\n  ctx->qlogfd = -1;\n  Curl_vquic_tls_cleanup(&ctx->tls);\n  vquic_ctx_free(&ctx->q);\n  if(ctx->h3conn) {\n    nghttp3_conn_del(ctx->h3conn);\n    ctx->h3conn = NULL;\n  }\n  if(ctx->qconn) {\n    ngtcp2_conn_del(ctx->qconn);\n    ctx->qconn = NULL;\n  }\n#ifdef OPENSSL_QUIC_API2\n  if(ctx->ossl_ctx) {\n    ngtcp2_crypto_ossl_ctx_del(ctx->ossl_ctx);\n    ctx->ossl_ctx = NULL;\n  }\n#endif\n  ctx->call_data = save;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode cf_ngtcp2_shutdown(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                   struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct cf_call_data save;\n  struct pkt_io_ctx pktx;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n\n  if(cf->shutdown || !ctx->qconn) {\n    *done = TRUE;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n\n  CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n  *done = FALSE;\n  pktx_init(&pktx, cf, data);\n\n  if(!ctx->shutdown_started) {\n    char buffer[NGTCP2_MAX_UDP_PAYLOAD_SIZE];\n    ngtcp2_ssize nwritten;\n\n    if(!Curl_bufq_is_empty(&ctx->q.sendbuf)) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"shutdown, flushing sendbuf\");\n      result = cf_progress_egress(cf, data, &pktx);\n      if(!Curl_bufq_is_empty(&ctx->q.sendbuf)) {\n        CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"sending shutdown packets blocked\");\n        result = CURLE_OK;\n        goto out;\n      }\n      else if(result) {\n        CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"shutdown, error %d flushing sendbuf\", result);\n        *done = TRUE;\n        goto out;\n      }\n    }\n\n    DEBUGASSERT(Curl_bufq_is_empty(&ctx->q.sendbuf));\n    ctx->shutdown_started = TRUE;\n    nwritten = ngtcp2_conn_write_connection_close(\n      ctx->qconn, NULL, /* path */\n      NULL, /* pkt_info */\n      (uint8_t *)buffer, sizeof(buffer),\n      &ctx->last_error, pktx.ts);\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"start shutdown(err_type=%d, err_code=%\"\n                PRIu64 \") -> %d\", ctx->last_error.type,\n                ctx->last_error.error_code, (int)nwritten);\n    /* there are cases listed in ngtcp2 documentation where this call\n     * may fail. Since we are doing a connection shutdown as graceful\n     * as we can, such an error is ignored here. */\n    if(nwritten > 0) {\n      /* Ignore amount written. sendbuf was empty and has always room for\n       * NGTCP2_MAX_UDP_PAYLOAD_SIZE. It can only completely fail, in which\n       * case `result` is set non zero. */\n      size_t n;\n      result = Curl_bufq_write(&ctx->q.sendbuf, (const unsigned char *)buffer,\n                               (size_t)nwritten, &n);\n      if(result) {\n        CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"error %d adding shutdown packets to sendbuf, \"\n                    \"aborting shutdown\", result);\n        goto out;\n      }\n\n      ctx->q.no_gso = TRUE;\n      ctx->q.gsolen = (size_t)nwritten;\n      ctx->q.split_len = 0;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if(!Curl_bufq_is_empty(&ctx->q.sendbuf)) {\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"shutdown, flushing egress\");\n    result = vquic_flush(cf, data, &ctx->q);\n    if(result == CURLE_AGAIN) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"sending shutdown packets blocked\");\n      result = CURLE_OK;\n      goto out;\n    }\n    else if(result) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"shutdown, error %d flushing sendbuf\", result);\n      *done = TRUE;\n      goto out;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if(Curl_bufq_is_empty(&ctx->q.sendbuf)) {\n    /* Sent everything off. ngtcp2 seems to have no support for graceful\n     * shutdowns. So, we are done. */\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"shutdown completely sent off, done\");\n    *done = TRUE;\n    result = CURLE_OK;\n  }\nout:\n  CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save);\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_conn_close(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                 struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  bool done;\n  cf_ngtcp2_shutdown(cf, data, &done);\n}\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_close(struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct cf_call_data save;\n\n  CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n  if(ctx && ctx->qconn) {\n    cf_ngtcp2_conn_close(cf, data);\n    cf_ngtcp2_ctx_close(ctx);\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"close\");\n  }\n  cf->connected = FALSE;\n  CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save);\n}\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_destroy(struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"destroy\");\n  if(cf->ctx) {\n    cf_ngtcp2_close(cf, data);\n    cf_ngtcp2_ctx_free(cf->ctx);\n    cf->ctx = NULL;\n  }\n}\n\n#ifdef USE_OPENSSL\n/* The \"new session\" callback must return zero if the session can be removed\n * or non-zero if the session has been put into the session cache.\n */\nstatic int quic_ossl_new_session_cb(SSL *ssl, SSL_SESSION *ssl_sessionid)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx;\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n  ngtcp2_crypto_conn_ref *cref;\n\n  cref = (ngtcp2_crypto_conn_ref *)SSL_get_app_data(ssl);\n  cf = cref ? cref->user_data : NULL;\n  ctx = cf ? cf->ctx : NULL;\n  data = cf ? CF_DATA_CURRENT(cf) : NULL;\n  if(cf && data && ctx) {\n    unsigned char *quic_tp = NULL;\n    size_t quic_tp_len = 0;\n#ifdef HAVE_OPENSSL_EARLYDATA\n    ngtcp2_ssize tplen;\n    uint8_t tpbuf[256];\n\n    tplen = ngtcp2_conn_encode_0rtt_transport_params(ctx->qconn, tpbuf,\n                                                     sizeof(tpbuf));\n    if(tplen < 0)\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"error encoding 0RTT transport data: %s\",\n                  ngtcp2_strerror((int)tplen));\n    else {\n      quic_tp = (unsigned char *)tpbuf;\n      quic_tp_len = (size_t)tplen;\n    }\n#endif\n    Curl_ossl_add_session(cf, data, ctx->peer.scache_key, ssl_sessionid,\n                          SSL_version(ssl), \"h3\", quic_tp, quic_tp_len);\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n#endif /* USE_OPENSSL */\n\n#ifdef USE_GNUTLS\n\n#ifdef CURLVERBOSE\nstatic const char *gtls_hs_msg_name(int mtype)\n{\n  switch(mtype) {\n  case 1:\n    return \"ClientHello\";\n  case 2:\n    return \"ServerHello\";\n  case 4:\n    return \"SessionTicket\";\n  case 8:\n    return \"EncryptedExtensions\";\n  case 11:\n    return \"Certificate\";\n  case 13:\n    return \"CertificateRequest\";\n  case 15:\n    return \"CertificateVerify\";\n  case 20:\n    return \"Finished\";\n  case 24:\n    return \"KeyUpdate\";\n  case 254:\n    return \"MessageHash\";\n  }\n  return \"Unknown\";\n}\n#endif\n\nstatic int quic_gtls_handshake_cb(gnutls_session_t session, unsigned int htype,\n                                  unsigned when, unsigned int incoming,\n                                  const gnutls_datum_t *msg)\n{\n  ngtcp2_crypto_conn_ref *conn_ref = gnutls_session_get_ptr(session);\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = conn_ref ? conn_ref->user_data : NULL;\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf ? cf->ctx : NULL;\n\n  (void)msg;\n  (void)incoming;\n  if(when && cf && ctx) { /* after message has been processed */\n    struct Curl_easy *data = CF_DATA_CURRENT(cf);\n    DEBUGASSERT(data);\n    if(!data)\n      return 0;\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"SSL message: %s %s [%d]\",\n                incoming ? \"<-\" : \"->\", gtls_hs_msg_name(htype), htype);\n    switch(htype) {\n    case GNUTLS_HANDSHAKE_NEW_SESSION_TICKET: {\n      ngtcp2_ssize tplen;\n      uint8_t tpbuf[256];\n      unsigned char *quic_tp = NULL;\n      size_t quic_tp_len = 0;\n\n      tplen = ngtcp2_conn_encode_0rtt_transport_params(ctx->qconn, tpbuf,\n                                                       sizeof(tpbuf));\n      if(tplen < 0)\n        CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"error encoding 0RTT transport data: %s\",\n                    ngtcp2_strerror((int)tplen));\n      else {\n        quic_tp = (unsigned char *)tpbuf;\n        quic_tp_len = (size_t)tplen;\n      }\n      (void)Curl_gtls_cache_session(cf, data, ctx->peer.scache_key,\n                                    session, 0, \"h3\", quic_tp, quic_tp_len);\n      break;\n    }\n    default:\n      break;\n    }\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n#endif /* USE_GNUTLS */\n\n#ifdef USE_WOLFSSL\nstatic int wssl_quic_new_session_cb(WOLFSSL *ssl, WOLFSSL_SESSION *session)\n{\n  ngtcp2_crypto_conn_ref *conn_ref = wolfSSL_get_app_data(ssl);\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = conn_ref ?
